Japanese: １２月２９日　午後　３時　３分 成歩堂法律事務所
English: December 29, 3:03 PM Wright & Co. Law Offices

Japanese: あのさ、なるほどくん。
English: Umm... Nick?

Japanese: ん？
English: What is it?

Japanese: コクハクしちゃうけど、あたし ニガテなんだよねー、手品。
English: I've got a confession to make... I'm terrible at figuring out magic tricks...

Japanese: てじな？
English: Magic tricks?

Japanese: うん。ホラ、手品って タネがあるんでしょ、かならず。
English: Yep. Magic tricks are by their very definition tricks, right?

Japanese: でもね。あたし、見てて わかったためしがないもん。
English: But I can never figure out the tricks when I see them. I'm just not good at it...

Japanese: そりゃ、プロだから。タネが バレないようにやってるんだよ。
English: That's because the tricks are performed by pros. They do it so you can't guess the trick.

Japanese: でも、でも、この前はみちゃんに 見せてもらったのもスゴかったよ！
English: But but... The trick Pearly showed me was incredible!

Japanese: 春美ちゃんが？ ‥‥へえ。どんなの？
English: Pearls did a magic trick? Hmm... What kind of trick was it?

Japanese: あのね。親ユビが‥‥ なんと、取れちゃうんだよ！
English: Let's see. It looked like she pulled the end of her own thumb off!
Heh...

Japanese: まずね。左手の親ユビを 右手でニギるんだよ。
English: First she put her right thumb next to her left hand, and then it just separated!

Japanese: そのまま、えいっ、て モギ取っちゃうの。
English: She could move it up and down and everything! It was incredible!

Japanese: こんなふうに？ ‥‥えいっ。
English: Really? Was it kinda like this...?

Japanese: うおッ！　すごい！ なるほどくん、魔術師だッ！
English: WHAT!? WOW! HOW'D YOU DO THAT!? Nick, you're like a real magician!

Japanese: ‥‥と、まあこんなワケだから、
English: See this is why I just can't figure out magic... I'm no good at it...

Japanese: 空飛ぶ殺人犯の使った魔術なんて、 あたしムキじゃないな。
English: Especially hard tricks like flying away from the scene of a murder...

Japanese: 魔術なんて、タネを考えて 見るモノじゃないんだけどな‥‥。
English: You'll take all the fun out of magic if you keep trying to figure it out.

Japanese: 観葉植物のチャーリーくん。
English: Charley. A quite decorative plant.

Japanese: お。なるほどくん、 ちゃんと水、あげてるみたいだね。
English: Nick, you've been watering the plant everyday, right?

Japanese: どれどれ。あたしも ゴチソウしてあげようかな。水。
English: I've been thinking about watering it too.

Japanese: ‥‥だから、やりすぎだよ、 真宵ちゃん。
English: Maya, we don't want to drown poor Charley.

Japanese: さあ、もっと大きくなるんだよ チャーリーくん。
English: But he'd get bigger!

Japanese: （これ以上デカく なられてもなあ‥‥）
English: (Does she want the office to look like a tropical rain forest or something?)

Japanese: ムズカシイ法律の本が あざ笑うように並んでいる。
English: Difficult-looking legal books stand in a formidable row. They mock me.

Japanese: あ、それ。あたし今、 ベンキョウ中なんだよ。ホーリツ。
English: Hey! I'm reading that one! You know I'm studying the law now, right!?

Japanese: 本にシオリ、はさんであるから。 ダメだよ、動かしちゃ。
English: I put that bookmark on the page I was reading... Don't you dare move it!

Japanese: えッ！　読んでるの？　コレを！
English: What!? You're reading!? THESE!?

Japanese: ‥‥シオリ、シオリ‥‥って、 コレ？　２ページ目に挟まってる。
English: A bookmark, huh... Hmm... You mean this thing? It was only on the second page.

Japanese: まあね。
English: Well, you have to start somewhere right?

Japanese: でも、１０００ページ以上 あるぞ、この本。
English: You do realize that you have 1000 pages to go right?

Japanese: やっぱり、売りはらっちゃおうか。 なるほどくん。
English: Maybe you really should sell all these books, Nick.

Japanese: 窓の向かいがわには、 巨大なビルがそびえている。
English: There is a giant building just outside the window.

Japanese: “ホテル・バンドー” ゴウカな高級ホテルだ。
English: It's the Gatewater Hotel, a high-class, luxury hotel.

Japanese: 最近じゃ、けっこうデカい イベントもやってるらしいよ。
English: Lately they've been holding huge events at the Gatewater.

Japanese: すっかり一流に なっちゃったねー。
English: It seems they've really become a top-class hotel.

Japanese: あそこのボーイさん、おぼえて いるかな？　あたしたちのこと。
English: I wonder if that bellboy would remember us?

Japanese: ぼくのデスクだ。
English: My desk.

Japanese: あまり座る機会がないため、 いやにキチンとかたづいている。
English: Since I don't have time to sit, it's unusually clean...

Japanese: ‥‥すっかりホコリかぶってるよ。 もうちょっと座ろうよ。
English: Umm... It's covered with dust. Maybe you should use your desk every now and again.

Japanese: テレビのヒーロー “トノサマン”のポスター。
English: A poster of the newest member of the "Steel Samurai" universe.

Japanese: 知ってる？　知ってる？ “トノサマン・ザ・ムービー！”
English: You know about the new "Steel Samurai" movie they are making, don't you Nick?

Japanese: ああ、なんか今度 映画化されたらしいね。
English: Yeah, I heard that they're making a movie starring the new cast.

Japanese: ゼッサン上映中だよ なるほどくん。
English: It's already out! Critics say, "Time to commit 'hara-kiri' if you miss this action epic!"

Japanese: へえ。
English: You don't say...

Japanese: 今年の“ヒーロー・グランプリ”は トノサマンでキマリだろう、って！
English: You know, I bet the star will win this year's Hero of Heroes Grand Prix!!

Japanese: ふうん。
English: *yawn* Uh huh...

Japanese: ‥‥わかったよ。今度 見に行くから。
English: Alright already... I'll go see the movie!!

Japanese: よーし。 早く事件、カイケツしようね！
English: Yay! Let's hurry and wrap this case up so we can see it!

Japanese: それにしても‥‥困ったね。
English: I don't see any way out of this. We're stuck between a rock and a hard place.

Japanese: 明日までに、犯人が空を飛んだ ナゾを解く、なんてねえ‥‥。
English: There's no way we can solve the mystery of a criminal flying by tomorrow...

Japanese: あたし思うんだけど。犯人て、 ホントに空なんか飛んだのかなあ。
English: I was thinking... Do you really think the criminal flew through the air?

Japanese: トミーさんだけでしょ？ “見た”って言ってるの。
English: The only one who thinks that is Moe, right? He says that he saw it.

Japanese: そうなんだけど‥‥。
English: That's true...

Japanese: あのときのトミーさん、ものすごく 真剣だったしなあ‥‥。
English: Moe looked awfully serious when he said that.

Japanese: マックスに罪を 着せたかった、とか‥‥？
English: Do you think he might be trying to frame Max?

Japanese: それなら、“彼を見た” って言うだけでじゅうぶんだよ。
English: If that was it, he would have simply said "I saw Max". That would have been enough.

Japanese: “飛びました”なんて、 信用されるワケないだろ？
English: There's no reason why anyone would believe "he flew".

Japanese: でも‥‥犯人がもし、ホントに 飛んだとしたら‥‥
English: But... What if the criminal really did fly?

Japanese: そんなコトできるの、マックスしか いないよ‥‥？
English: There's only one person who could do that... Max.

Japanese: （そ、そうなっちゃうのかな‥‥）
English: (You're really not helping my confidence here, Maya...)

Japanese: ベンさんもリロくんもトミーさんも マックスのコト、キライみたい。
English: Everyone seems to hate Max. Ben, Trilo, Moe... They all have nothing nice to say.

Japanese: うーん‥‥。あの魔術師、 あからさまにエラそうだもんな。
English: That magician does seem rather full of himself, doesn't he?

Japanese: “オレがいるから客が入るんだ” ‥‥って、ハナにかけてるし。
English: Especially when he says things like customers only go to the circus to see him.

Japanese: ベンさんなんて、ビンで アタマなぐられたしね。
English: He even hit Ben over the head with a bottle.

Japanese: ‥‥でも。
English: But...
But what?

Japanese: そんなことぐらいで、 サツジンの罪なんて着せないよ！
English: But that doesn't seem enough to frame another person for murder!!

Japanese: そうは言っても、２人とも ウソはついていないぞ。
English: And it was proven that neither Ben nor Moe lied on the stand.

Japanese: ベンさんが見たのは、マックスの 衣装を着た団長さんだった‥‥。
English: Maybe Ben saw the Ringmaster wearing Max's costume,

Japanese: でも、トミーさんはハッキリ 見てるんだよね‥‥マックス。
English: but Moe clearly said that he saw Max himself.

Japanese: ‥‥かろうじて、まだ 光ってるね、それ。
English: Well... Your badge is still shining. Barely.

Japanese: もう１、２年もすると、 すっかりくすんじゃうね。
English: It may be showing a bit of wear, but I try to take good care of this thing.

Japanese: それにしてもなるほどくん、 ホントそれ見せるの好きだね。
English: You sure do love to show that off, don't you Nick?

Japanese: あたしなんて、何度 見せつけられたことか。
English: Who knows how many times you have shown it to me?

Japanese: （たしかに。‥‥つい 見せちゃうんだよな‥‥）
English: (She's right... I whip it out at the drop of a hat.)

Japanese: あ。そうそう。 ‥‥この写真だけどさ。
English: Oh yeah... The picture of the crime scene.

Japanese: この木箱、なんなのかな。
English: I wonder what that wooden box is?

Japanese: たぶん、現場に 置いてあったんだろうね。
English: It was probably left at the scene.

Japanese: 中には、コショウのビンが 入っていただけ、か。
English: I can't believe there was only a bottle of pepper in that thing.

Japanese: いやー、これがどう 事件にカラんでくるのか？
English: It must be related to the crime somehow...

Japanese: 楽しみだねえ、なるほどくん。
English: This is fun, isn't it Nick?

Japanese: いやいやいや。それを ぼくたちで考えるんだよ。
English: Not really... It may be fun to solve mysteries, but don't forget this is a murder case.

Japanese: うううう‥‥ あたしにはダメっぽいや。
English: Awww... You're right. I should take things more seriously.

Japanese: どこからどう見ても マックスのシルクハットだねー。
English: No matter how you look at it, this is definitely Max's silk hat.

Japanese: ハネがついてるしなあ。
English: There's even a feather stuck in it.

Japanese: このぼうしも、なんか モンダイがあったよね。
English: There's something wrong with this hat though.

Japanese: トミーさんの証言だな。
English: Moe's testimony, right?

Japanese: “犯人は、それをかぶったまま 夜空に消えた”、って。
English: "He left the scene wearing that dumb silk hat!"

Japanese: ‥‥ジッサイは、現場に コロがっていたのに。
English: That's what he said, but the hat was found on the ground at the scene.

Japanese: ううう‥‥ナゾだ。
English: Hmm... This really is a mystery.

Japanese: ううーん。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: どうしたの？
English: What's the matter, Maya?

Japanese: トノサマンのポスターとこれ、 どっちがいいかなあ？
English: Which poster is cooler? This one or the "Steel Samurai" poster?

Japanese: ぼくはどっちもイヤだけどな。
English: I'm not a big fan of either one of them.

Japanese: やっぱり、弁護士事務所に 魔術師はオカシイかな。
English: I guess putting a poster of a magician in a law office is kinda strange.

Japanese: （トノサマンのほうが もっとヘンだろ！）
English: (No stranger than a poster of a hunk of junk carrying around a spear!)

Japanese: ‥‥これを見ると、 シンケンに考えちゃうよねー。
English: Looking at this really makes you think about things, doesn't it?

Japanese: 何が？
English: Like what?

Japanese: 霊媒師なんかやってたって、 こんなにギャラ、もらえないもん。
English: No matter how great a medium I become, there is no way I could make this kind of money.

Japanese: ギャラ、ほしいの？
English: So it's money you're after?

Japanese: 一度きりの人生だからね。 ゴージャスに行きたいよ！
English: You only live life once, right? Well, then you might as well live it fabulously!

Japanese: いやー、ギャラ３カ月ぶんだよ、 ３カ月ぶん。
English: Can you believe this cost three months of pay? Three months!!

Japanese: アイだねー、リロくんの。
English: Trilo doesn't skimp when it comes to Regina. He's really in love.

Japanese: 本格的なガラス玉らしいからな。 たしかにピカピカ光ってる。
English: It looks like a real glass stone. Its brilliance is amazing.

Japanese: こりゃたしかに、ルーサーくんの 大好物だね。
English: I bet Money would love to get his hands on this again.

Japanese: （とりあえず、今度 返してあげるか‥‥）
English: (I'll have to give this back to Trilo sometime...)

Japanese: ひゃあ。マックス、これで 殴ったの？　ベンさん。
English: Whoa. Max used a broken bottle to crack Ben over the head?

Japanese: ちがうよ。殴った結果 ビンが割れて、こうなったんだ。
English: No no no... It broke because Max cracked Ben over the head with it.

Japanese: きっと、思いっきり 殴ったんだねー。
English: He must have really hit him hard then...

Japanese: ‥‥よし。 ちょっと、やってみようか。
English: I guess I should give it a try... For research purposes. Come here...

Japanese: ちょうどここに、ビンがあるし。
English: I've got a nice bottle right here for you Nick...

Japanese: ぎゃッ！
English: YEOW!!

Japanese: うーん。割れないねえ。
English: That's weird. It didn't break.

Japanese: ‥‥ペットボトルが 割れるかよ！
English: Of course it didn't!! You hit me with a plastic bottle!!

Japanese: １０キロ‥‥って、 ちょっと重いよね。
English: Twenty pounds, huh? That's pretty heavy.

Japanese: 箱の中には、コショウのビン が入ってただけ。
English: Especially since the only thing inside was a small bottle of pepper.

Japanese: ‥‥ううう、考えるほど アタマがいたくなってくるよ。
English: The more you think about it the more it starts to make your head hurt.

Japanese: ま、明日になれば、イイ思い出に なってると思うけどさ。
English: But compared to tomorrow this will all be a happy memory.

Japanese: 早すぎるよ！
English: Tomorrow!? Don't get ahead of yourself Maya... Let's concentrate on today.

Japanese: ああ、これこれ。
English: You wanna know something Nick?

Japanese: よく、ラーメンにコショウ 入れたり、
English: I love to put pepper on my hamburgers.

Japanese: スイカにシオふったり するヒト、いるでしょ？
English: But I also like to put pepper on peanut butter and jelly sandwiches.

Japanese: あたしはどうかと 思うねー、そういうの。
English: Do you think I'm weird?

Japanese: なんか今日は、ノリが悪いね、 なるほどくん。
English: You don't seem to be in a very good mood today.

Japanese: ‥‥そりゃそうだよ。
English: Of course I'm not...

Japanese: 空飛ぶ犯人を見つけなくちゃ ならないからなあ。
English: I have to find a criminal who has discovered the ability to fly.

Japanese: はああ、そうだよねー。
English: Yeah. I guess that would put you in a bad mood.

Japanese: ‥‥これなんだけど。
English: What about this?

Japanese: うーん‥‥そうだねえ‥‥。
English: Umm... Yeah...

Japanese: ゴメンね。なんにも 思いつかないや。
English: Sorry. I got nothing.

Japanese: （まさか、ギャグを考えて いたんじゃないだろうな‥‥）
English: (She must have been trying to think up a joke or something.)

Japanese: トミーさんが目撃した “空飛ぶ犯人”って、やっぱり‥‥
English: If Moe says that he saw a flying criminal, that means it must have been...

Japanese: まさか。ヒトが飛ぶわけないだろ？
English: What's the matter with you!? You know that people can't just up and fly!

Japanese: だって、マックスは魔術師だよ？
English: But Max... He is a magician.

Japanese: タネがなきゃ、 魔術は成立しないよ。
English: Without their bag of tricks, magicians are incapable of magic.

Japanese: わざわざあの現場に シカケを作るイミ、ないだろ？
English: Besides, what's the likelihood someone set up a flying device at the crime scene? Near zero.

Japanese: トミーさんに見せるため、とか。
English: What if they set something up so they could trick Moe?

Japanese: ううん‥‥なんか、 ムダな気がするけどなあ‥‥。
English: Umm... Maybe. But I get the feeling that would have been pointless...

Japanese: 霊媒師なんてやってるとさ。
English: Once you start channeling spirits...

Japanese: 本当の自分はいったいダレなんだ！ ‥‥なんて。
English: They say you lose sight of who you actually are...

Japanese: へえ‥‥。 そんなコト、思うの？
English: Hmm... Do you really believe that?

Japanese: ベツに思わないけど。
English: Not really. But...

Japanese: ちょっとイミありげでしょ。 そのうち、自伝に書こうと思って。
English: Just in case, I plan to write my autobiography.

Japanese: じでん‥‥？
English: Your autobiography...?

Japanese: それにしても、いっつも この刑事だよなあ、現場で会うの。
English: No matter what the crime, we always meet the same detective at the scene.

Japanese: もしかしたらさあ。ひとりしか いないのかもね。刑事さん。
English: I bet that's because he's the only detective on the force.

Japanese: だとしたら、すごい エリートだよ、イトノコさん。
English: That would make Gumshoe really elite, wouldn't it?

Japanese: （どう見ても、エリートって ガラじゃないけどね‥‥）
English: (Something tells me that no matter how you look at it, he's anything but elite...)

Japanese: あれで、ズルいコトしなきゃ 好きになれるんだけどな。
English: You know, if she wasn't so mean, I think she and I would get along...

Japanese: “ズルいコト”じゃすまない モンダイだよ、あれは。
English: Yeah, but that's the problem... She is so mean.

Japanese: それに、あのムチ。 けっこうイタいぞあれは。
English: And that whip... That thing hurts. It really hurts.

Japanese: カッコいいよねー、ムチ。 あたしもほしいな。
English: It is kinda cool though. I wouldn't mind having a whip myself.

Japanese: なるほどくんを 思うさまたたいてみたい。
English: I bet that cracking the whip on you once or twice would be just what the doctor ordered!

Japanese: な、なんでぼくなんだよ‥‥。
English: W-Why me!? W-What did I do!?

Japanese: ‥‥団長さん、だれからも 好かれてるよね。
English: Everyone loved the Ringmaster...

Japanese: たしかに。
English: So I'm told.

Japanese: どうして殺されなきゃ ならなかったんだろう‥‥。
English: Why would anyone want to kill such a beloved man of the big top?

Japanese: ケッキョク、まだわからないね。 この事件のドーキ。
English: We still don't know what motivated the crime.

Japanese: （明日までに‥‥わかるといいな）
English: (It'd be good to have some inkling of the motive before court tomorrow...)

Japanese: いいよね、なんか腹話術って。
English: Being a ventriloquist sounds like an interesting job.

Japanese: できたら楽しいとは 思うけどね。
English: I bet it's fun once you get good at it.

Japanese: ね。ね。あたし、 ちょっとやってみようか。
English: Maybe I should give it a try then!

Japanese: いくよ！
English: Here I go!

Japanese: “こんにちは。 ぼく、なるほどくん”
English: "Hello e-e-everyone... I-I-I'm Phoenix Wright."

Japanese: “どんなウソでも 見やぶってやるぅ！”
English: "No matter what the lie or who the liar, I'll expose them for what they are!"
What's the verdict?

Japanese: 動きまくってるよ、クチ。
English: You might want to practice not moving your lips so much.

Japanese: ううん。‥‥ちょっと、 修行してみようかなあ。
English: Y-Yeah... Maybe I should sign up for some expert training.

Japanese: （霊媒のほうは どうするんだ‥‥？）
English: (Don't you have another type of training to attend to, young lady...?)

Japanese: いいヒトだよね、トミーさん。
English: Moe is a really nice guy, don't you think Nick?

Japanese: サーカスのこと、 いっしょうけんめい考えてるし。
English: He taught us all about the inner workings of the circus.

Japanese: そうだな。ぼくも、ちょっと 見なおしたよ。
English: Yep. It definitely made me look at things in a new light.

Japanese: それに、あのギャグセンス。 ふるえるよねー、なるほどくん！
English: And he's got such a great sense of humor. It tickles your funny bone, doesn't it?

Japanese: （‥‥そこで意見が わかれるんだよな）
English: (Well... I can't argue that some people might find it funny...)

Japanese: （たしかに、ふるえることは ふるえるんだけど‥‥）
English: (But the only thing it tickles on me is my desire for high quality earplugs...)

Japanese: かわいいよねー、ミリカちゃん。
English: Regina is sooo cute!

Japanese: たしかに、なんだか異様な チカラがあるな、あの笑顔。
English: I have to admit that her smile does have a sort of hypnotic power.

Japanese: しかも、猛獣使い。 ‥‥カッコイイなあ。
English: Can you believe that she's an animal tamer? That's sooo cool!

Japanese: あたしもちょっと やってみたい。
English: I'd like to try animal taming someday.

Japanese: ホレ、なるほどくん！ おて！　おまわり！
English: Nick! Nick! Lie down! Roll over!!

Japanese: この事件が解決したら、 いくらでもまわってあげるよ。
English: Once this case is solved, I'll roll over for you as many times as you want!

Japanese: わあい！　ヤクソクだよ！
English: YAY!!! Remember, you promised, Nick!

Japanese: たいしたヤツじゃないね。
English: That's not really important is it?

Japanese: ‥‥そんなこと言うなよ。
English: Must you always be so blunt?

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 留置所・面会室
English: December 29 Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: おお。ハニイたち！
English: Oh! My sweeties!!

Japanese: ‥‥さあ、早くボクを ここから出しておくれよ！
English: You mind hurrying up and getting me out of this place!?

Japanese: ええ。 今、がんばってますから。
English: We're doing our best, Max. Just hang in there.

Japanese: ‥‥さっき、 テレビ局の連中が来てさあ。
English: A little while ago, some people from a local TV station came by...

Japanese: “セッカクですから、特別番組を 作りましょう”なんて言うんだよ。
English: They said that, since I'm a famed magician, "Let's make your very own TV special!"

Japanese: へええ。どんな番組ですか？
English: Really? What kind of TV special?

Japanese: “マックス・ギャラクティカ、 刑務所から奇跡の大脱出！”。
English: "Maximillion Galactica: The Great Prison Escape" It would be aired live.

Japanese: わ！　ちょっと オモシロそうですねー。
English: Hey! That sounds like it would be an awesome special!

Japanese: ‥‥でも、そんなコトしたら、 逆に出られなくなっちゃうだろ？
English: But if I do the special before I'm acquitted, they'll never let me out of here for real.

Japanese: よけいな罪をしょいこむのは まちがいないでしょうね。
English: Well, it would surely be an unnecessary addition to your troubles with the law.

Japanese: なんか連中、イロイロ 企画してるらしくてさ。
English: That's what I was thinking... But the production staff is already working on the show!

Japanese: 早いトコ出してくれないと、 ダツゴクするハメになっちまう。
English: If you don't get me out quick, I'll have no choice but to stage a real prison break.

Japanese: ‥‥はあ。ノンキなもんですね‥‥
English: You seem awfully calm about that possibility...

Japanese: それがショー・ビジネスの 世界というものさ！
English: I'd have no choice. It would be a contractual obligation... That's show business.

Japanese: 面会のようすを監視する看守だ。
English: This guard monitors the visitor's room.

Japanese: 何か言いたげに こちらをジッと見ている。
English: He's so quiet, you could forget he was even there... If he wasn't watching everything.

Japanese: ‥‥うしろにかくしてるのは‥‥ 色紙とサインペンのようだ。
English: He's pushed up against the wall... Kinda like a magnet on a refrigerator.

Japanese: ごていねいに、カメラで 見張っているようだ。
English: Smile, you're on candid camera.

Japanese: マックスがたまに チラリと視線を送っている。
English: Max keeps sneaking glances up at the security camera on the wall...

Japanese: 芸能人の悲しいサガ みたいなものなのかな。
English: I wonder if he is still trying to be a star?

Japanese: あの。事件の夜は、ホントに 飛ばなかったんですね？　空。
English: Umm. The night of the crime... You didn't happen to fly off into the sky, did you?

Japanese: カンベンしてくれよハニイ。
English: Here is how everything went down, sweetie.

Japanese: ボクはあの時間、 団長の部屋にいたんだ。
English: At the time of the murder, I was sitting in the Ringmaster's room.

Japanese: それに‥‥
English: Not to mention...

Japanese: 空を飛ぶのは、けっこう メンドウなんだよ。
English: Flying off into the sky is not just something I can do at will.

Japanese: 観客がしなびたピエロ１人じゃあ、 ワリに合わないね。
English: I don't care what the stoogey clown says, it wasn't me.

Japanese: あの。あの。その魔術のトリック、 教えてもらえませんか？
English: Max! Max! Do you mind teaching me the trick behind flying?

Japanese: ‥‥う。そ、それはカンベン してほしいなハニイ。
English: Hmm... You'll have to forgive me, sweetie.

Japanese: ボクは最近のヤワな手品師どもと ちがって、口がカタいからね。
English: The difference between me and cheap imitation magicians is that I keep my mouth shut.

Japanese: でも、これだけは言えるよ。 カンタンにできることじゃない。
English: I don't teach people tricks, but I will say this much. It's much harder than you think.

Japanese: 今日の法廷を聞いてて 思ったんですけど。
English: I was thinking about this in court today... I've got a favor to ask of you.

Japanese: なんだい、ハニイ。
English: Anything for you, sweetie.

Japanese: もっと仲よくしましょうよ！ サーカスのヒトたちと。
English: Be friends with the other performers in the circus!

Japanese: ゴージャス！　どうしてボクが、 あんな二流芸人どもと！
English: FABULOUS! A great joke!! Why would I be friends with a bunch of hacks like that?

Japanese: でも‥‥！
English: But...!

Japanese: ボクはね。世界のステージで グランプリを取ってるんだよ！
English: I've won on the world stage! I won the International Grand Prix!

Japanese: グランプリ‥‥ですか。
English: International Grand Prix?

Japanese: 芸人はね、“世界”に 目を向けるべきなんだ！
English: Performers should always look to perform on the world stage!

Japanese: 特に、ここのサーカスの連中、 向上心がなさすぎる！
English: But the performers at this circus are completely and utterly devoid of ambition!

Japanese: ボクには、それが どうしてもユルせないんだ！
English: That is something that I can simply not tolerate!
(Ambition, huh...?)

Japanese: マックスの言うことも、 なんか正しい気がしてきた‥‥。
English: Something about what Max just said rings true to my ears...

Japanese: おやおや。グランプリの話、 ハニイたちも聞きたいのかい？
English: Oh my! My sweeties want to hear all about the Grand Prix, don't they?

Japanese: ‥‥もう１００回も話したから、 ウンザリなんだけどね。
English: To be honest though, I've told this story like 100 times already, so it's a bit boring.

Japanese: はあ、スミマセン。
English: We're sorry to make you tell it again.

Japanese: やれやれ、ホントにマッタク、 この話、もうタクサンなんだけど。
English: You must not have heard me... I am really sick of telling this story.

Japanese: しかたないな。 ハニイのたのみとあってはね。
English: But what can you do? I'm Maximillion Galactica. I suppose I can tell it again.

Japanese: ‥‥ホラ！　ホラ！ 見てくれたまえ、コイツを！
English: Voila! Here, take a look at this!

Japanese: グーゼンにも今、そのときの 写真を持ってるんだよ！
English: I just happen to have a picture from the Grand Prix with me.

Japanese: 見てくれたまえ。 このゴージャスなステージ！
English: Just look at that fabulous stage!!

Japanese: ここでね。初めて飛んだんだよ。 客席の上を、スイーッとね！
English: That is the first stage that I ever flew on. I flew right over the audience!

Japanese: みんな、もう大カッサイだぁ。
English: The crowd erupted into applause!

Japanese: オラもう、死んでもイイと 思っただ。あの瞬間、ホンキで！
English: At that time, I thought to myself that I could die right then and die a happy man.

Japanese: ‥‥あのカンドーとコーフン、 オラ一生忘れね。忘れるもんかぁ！
English: I'll never forget how I felt that night. The emotions... The acclaim...

Japanese: へえええ。
English: Wow.

Japanese: コホン。 ‥‥と、とにかく。
English: *ahem* By the way...

Japanese: 芸人になった以上、ワレワレは あのカンドーを味わうべきなんだ！
English: I think everyone who is a performer should get to experience that feeling.

Japanese: このサーカスの連中に、ボクは それを教えてやりたいね！
English: I just wish I could explain that to the other people in the circus!

Japanese: ‥‥すごいなあ。 あたしもほしいな、トロフィー。
English: That's incredible Max... I want a trophy too!

Japanese: ね。なるほどくん。買って。
English: Hey Nick! How about you buy me a trophy!?

Japanese: ‥‥そういうもんじゃないだろ、 トロフィーって。
English: That's not how you earn a trophy Maya!

Japanese: ハニイたち、 その写真はあげるからさあ‥‥、
English: My sweeties, you can have this picture of my triumph...

Japanese: サーカスの連中に、よおおく 見せてやっておくれよ！
English: Just make sure you show it to all the other members of the circus.

Japanese: “ボクを見ならいたまえ！” ってコトで。
English: "Watch and learn!" That's what you should tell them.

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥）
English: (Learn how to get thrown in jail?)

Japanese: 証拠品《グランプリの写真》を 法廷記録に挟んだ。
English: Grand Prix Photo added to the Court Record.

Japanese: う。‥‥こ、これは ‥‥団長。
English: That's... That's the Ringmaster.

Japanese: おキノドクだ。
English: It's a shame what happened...

Japanese: ボクのパパになるはずの ヒトだったのに‥‥。
English: He was going to be like a father to me...

Japanese: たしかにコレは ぼくのシルクハットだよ。
English: This is definitely my silk hat.

Japanese: さすがに、もう かぶるワケにもいかないケドね。
English: I don't suppose I'm allowed to wear it anymore am I?

Japanese: マックスさんの、キザ‥‥イヤ、 シンボルの１つなんですよね。
English: Max, this one of your uppi... I mean, excellent symbols, right?

Japanese: ‥‥ああ。このハネのかざり、 気に入ってたんだけどね‥‥。
English: That's right. Honestly though, I only like it because of the feather in the brim.

Japanese: いつ見てもホレボレするね。 マックス・ギャラクティカ。
English: Maximillion Galactica! No matter when you see me, you get shivers don't you?

Japanese: シルクハットにマント、そして ムネの白いバラ。
English: The silk hat... The cloak... The white roses on my chest...

Japanese: ‥‥ボクのシンボルさ。 やっと、３つにしぼったんだ。
English: They're my symbols... You know I had to cut the number of symbols to three?

Japanese: 最初はいくつ あったんですか？
English: Really? You had quite a few to begin with then, huh?

Japanese: いや、多いほうがいいと 思ってさ。
English: Well, I thought that you could never have too many symbols...

Japanese: サングラスにホクロ、ヒゲに 出っ歯にタラコくちびる‥‥
English: Sunglasses... Beauty mark... Soft pillows for lips... A beard... Buck teeth...

Japanese: 各種シンボルを取りそろえて いたんだけど。
English: I gave all sorts of symbols a shot.

Japanese: メイクのときに忘れると、 ダレだかわからなくなるんでね。
English: But if I forgot one when I put on my makeup, everyone would forget who I am!

Japanese: タイヘンですねー、魔術師も。
English: That's awful... Especially for a magician.

Japanese: ダメですよ！　マックスさん。 ベンさんを殴って‥‥。
English: Max! How could you!? How could you hit poor Ben over the head with this!?

Japanese: うう‥‥さすがにハンセイ してるよ。
English: I've been thinking about that lately...

Japanese: アイツ、このボクをキザな イカサマヤロー呼ばわりして‥‥
English: But he did call me a "no-good talentless hack of a fast food magician"...

Japanese: それって‥‥やっぱり、 リロくんが？
English: You mean Trilo said that? Not Ben, right?

Japanese: “リロ”も“ベン”もない。 木住　勉はヒトリだよ。
English: There is no Trilo or Ben. There is only one Benjamin Woodman.

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥ そうなんですよねえ‥‥。
English: Hmm... I guess you're right.

Japanese: ただでさえゴージャスなボクが、 世界で一番かがやいた瞬間だね。
English: I'm normally fabulous, but in this picture, I'm the king of the world!

Japanese: ゴージャスなトロフィーと 胸像をもらったんだよ！
English: I even got a fabulous trophy and a super fabulous bust.

Japanese: カッコイイなあ。あたし、 見てみたい！　トロフィー。
English: That's really cool! I wouldn't mind seeing your trophy some day.

Japanese: ああ。それなら 食堂に行ってみたまえよ。
English: If you want to see one of my awards, you should just go to the cafeteria.

Japanese: ‥‥食堂？　って、あの‥‥ サーカスのテントの中にある？
English: The cafeteria? You mean the one inside the circus tent?

Japanese: モチロン。トロフィーはないけど、 胸像がかざってあるハズさ。
English: Of course. The trophy isn't there, but the bust that I won is!

Japanese: 食堂なら、 きのう、行ってみたんだけどな。
English: It's in the cafeteria? We were just there yesterday.

Japanese: なるほどくん、キョーゾーなんて 置いてあったっけ？
English: Nick, did you see a bust anywhere inside the cafeteria?

Japanese: ‥‥あの。この張り紙、 ご存じですか？
English: Do you know anything about this note?

Japanese: 事件の朝、食堂に張って あったんですけど‥‥
English: The morning of the murder, it was posted on the wall in the cafeteria...

Japanese: ああ、モチロン知ってるとも。 さすがのボクもビックリしたよ。
English: I do know all about that note. When I read it, my heart certainly skipped a beat.

Japanese: ‥‥“ビックリ”？
English: Your heart skipped a beat?

Japanese: 朝のティーを楽しんでいたら、 団長たちが入ってきたんだよ。
English: While I was enjoying my morning tea, the Ringmaster and company entered the room.

Japanese: ‥‥団長たち？
English: And company...?

Japanese: 団長と、ボクの スウィート・ハニイさ！
English: I guess it wasn't really a company... It was just the Ringmaster and my sweetie pie.

Japanese: 団長、この張り紙を見たとたん、 真っ赤になってね。
English: When the Ringmaster read the note, he turned an incredible bright red.

Japanese: いきなりビリビリ破って、衣装の ポケットに突ッこんじまった！
English: All of a sudden he tore it off the wall and shoved it into the pocket of his tailcoat!

Japanese: えええっ！
English: Really!?

Japanese: ‥‥いったい、 何が書いてあったんですか！
English: Out of curiosity, what in the world was written on that thing?

Japanese: ‥‥さあね。
English: Let's see...

Japanese: うううう。
English: Uh-huh...

Japanese: すまないがハニイたち、 自分で探してみてくれたまえ。
English: Oh I don't want to steal the fun from my sweeties! Go and find out on your own!

Japanese: まだ、どこかに あるかもしれないよ。
English: I'm sure you can find it somewhere.

Japanese: スウィート・ハニイにも 聞いてみたほうがいいかもね！
English: You might also want to ask my sweetie pie princess.

Japanese: あの、これなんですけど‥‥。
English: What about this?

Japanese: ゴージャス！　このボクが、 そんなものにサインすると思うか？
English: Fabulous!! You'd like me to sign this for you?

Japanese: （してもらおうとも思わないよ！）
English: (*sigh*)

Japanese: どうだい？　今度、 ボクのステージに来ないかい？
English: So, would you like to come up on stage with me sometime?

Japanese: え！　いいんですか！
English: Re-Really? Is that really OK?

Japanese: お客さん代表として、 ステージに上げてあげるよ。
English: I'll bring you up on stage as a volunteer from the audience!

Japanese: ホントにッ！
English: REALLY!?

Japanese: で、前もってわたしておいた ネタを、こっそりボクに‥‥
English: I'll give you something before the show, and then on stage you'll sneak it to me...

Japanese: （‥‥それ、 サクラじゃないか！）
English: (Wait a second... He's planting her in the audience to rig a magic trick!)

Japanese: あの、これなんですけど‥‥。
English: Do you mind taking a look at this?

Japanese: はぁ。しかたないな。 ‥‥‥‥‥‥ホレ。
English: I guess I have no choice... But really, is this the proper time or place for this?

Japanese: （写真にサイン書かれた‥‥）
English: (He signed his own photo...)

Japanese: 事件が解決したら ちょうだいね、それ。
English: Nick... You mind giving that to me when the case is over?

Japanese: ‥‥う、うーん。 ちょっと、こういうのはね。
English: U-Uh-huh... This woman, huh?

Japanese: やっぱりボクにふさわしいのは、 ふんわりやさしいハニイさ。
English: Well... My type of woman is a gentle sweetie pie... Let that mean what it means.

Japanese: はあ。ミリカちゃんに ゾッコンなんだねー。
English: Sounds like you are describing Regina!

Japanese: （‥‥というより、この視線に ビビってるだけじゃないか？）
English: (Well, he's definitely not describing Franziska!)

Japanese: ‥‥ま。ここだけのハナシ。
English: Just between you and I...

Japanese: ワルいヒトではなかった。 ‥‥これだけは言えるよ。
English: He's wasn't a bad guy at all. I can say that for sure...

Japanese: マックスがヒトを ほめるの、はじめて聞いたね。
English: That's the first time I've ever heard you say something nice about someone, Max.

Japanese: きっと、ホントにいい人 だったんじゃないかな。
English: He must have really been a nice guy...

Japanese: おお、ボクのスウィート・ハニイ。
English: Ahhh... My sweetie pie princess.

Japanese: ボクがこんなことになって、 さぞさびしがっていただろうね。
English: She must be really lonely with me all cooped up in here.

Japanese: （ニコニコしてトラと たわむれてたけど‥‥）
English: (Actually, she was laughing and rolling around with her tiger...)

Japanese: 彼女は、ボクのトクベツなんだ。 そしてボクは、彼女のトクベツ。
English: She is my special someone. And I am her special someone.

Japanese: うーん。‥‥フクザツですねえ。
English: Uh-huh... Very complex...

Japanese: この人物なんですけど‥‥。
English: What about this person?

Japanese: ワルいけど。‥‥ボク、 弟子はとらない主義でね。
English: Unfortunately I don't take on apprentices.

Japanese: だいいち、見こみなさそうだよ そのツラは。
English: I mean, just look at the face on this one... It's not what I'd call fabulous.

Japanese: （ヒドい言われようだな‥‥）
English: (That's awfully harsh...)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 留置所・面会室
English: December 29 Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: マックス、また取り調べかな。
English: They must have taken Max in for questioning again.

Japanese: とりあえず、今のところ 彼に聞くことはないからね。
English: There really isn't anything that we need to ask him right now anyway.

Japanese: そうだねえ‥‥。 じゃあ、いいか。
English: You're right, I guess... Alright, let's go then.

Japanese: 面会のようすを監視する看守だ。
English: This guard monitors the visitor's room.

Japanese: タイクツなのだろうか。 こっちには見向きもしない。
English: I wonder if he is bored? He doesn't even try to glance over this way...

Japanese: ごていねいに、カメラで 見張っているようだ。
English: Smile, you're on candid camera.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 タチミ・サーカス　正門前
English: December 29 Berry Big Circus Circus Entrance

Japanese: ？　‥‥なんか、ボソボソ 声が聞こえるよ‥‥？
English: You hear that? It sounds like two people arguing...

Japanese: ‥‥よし、行くぜ。 ジュンビはいいか？
English: Alright, let's do it. Are you ready?

Japanese: う、うん‥‥。 あ、お‥‥で。でも‥‥
English: Y-Yes... A-Ah... Wait...

Japanese: つべこべ言うなよ！ とにかく、やるしかねえんだよ。
English: Quit your whining! Let's just give this a shot already!

Japanese: ‥‥さん、はい！
English: Alright! Let's go!

Japanese: かーえーるーのーうーたーが。
English: Row-row-row-your-boat!

Japanese: かーえーるーのーうーたーが。
English: Row-row-row-your-boat!

Japanese: ナニやってんだよ！
English: What are you doing!?

Japanese: “きーこーえーてー くーるーよ”だろうが！
English: Gently-down-the-stream! C'mon, you know that!!

Japanese: そ、それはわ。わかってるケド。 や。やっぱりムリだよリロくん‥‥
English: I'm trying my best, but... Trilo, this just isn't going to work...

Japanese: バカ言ってんじゃねえよ。 これからは“個性”の時代だぜ！
English: Do you enjoy saying dumb things? You're going to have to be on your own someday!

Japanese: これぐらいできなくて、 どうすんだよ！
English: If you can't handle something as simple as this, what are you going to do then!?

Japanese: あのー。 こんにちは。ベンさん、リロくん。
English: Hello Ben. Hello to you too, Trilo.

Japanese: コラァッ！　ヒミツ特訓中だぞ！ 聞いてんじゃねえよ！
English: What are you doing here!? Can't you see we're on a secret crash training course!?

Japanese: ご、ごめんなさい‥‥。
English: I'm sorry...

Japanese: トックン‥‥って、まさか。
English: Secret crash training! Whoa.

Japanese: え。ええ‥‥。リロくんが、 どうしてもやりたいって‥‥
English: Yes. Trilo wouldn't give up until I said we'd try out his idea for a new routine.

Japanese: その。り。輪唱を‥‥
English: So... We were trying to sing in a round for our new ventriloquism act.

Japanese: りんしょう‥‥って。 できるんですか！　そんなコト。
English: In a round!? You can really do that!? That's incredible!

Japanese: 見ろよ！　ベン！ コイツら、ビックリしてるぜ！
English: See! See! Even they are surprised by the idea!! I told you!!

Japanese: ‥‥そりゃ、ボクだって ビックリするよ‥‥
English: They're not the only ones!! You even surprised me with your idea!!

Japanese: ネタのツカミはオッケーだ！ あとは、練習あるのみだな！
English: Once we've got a grip on the basics, then it's just a matter of practice!

Japanese: そ、そそ。そそそ、そんな‥‥
English: Y-Y-You t-t-think so?

Japanese: あ。そうだ。 ‥‥返すね、これ。
English: Oh, I almost forgot! I wanted to give this back to you.

Japanese: おッ、それ！
English: Ahh! There it is!!

Japanese: ‥‥よーし。これでまた、 ミリカにアタックできるぜ！
English: Now that I've got this ring back, it's time to take one more shot at Regina!

Japanese: あの‥‥。今日の法廷で 証言したことなんですけど。
English: Umm... I know that you already testified in court today...

Japanese: ああ。オレたちが 見たヤツのコトだろ？
English: You want to talk about what we saw, right?

Japanese: うん。
English: Yes.

Japanese: たしかにさあ。最初は オヤジだと思ったワケよ。
English: Well, at first we thought it was the old man...

Japanese: 歩き方とか、フンイキとか。 ‥‥なあ、ベン！
English: Just looking at his walk and how he acted. Right Ben?

Japanese: う！　お。お。そ、そうだね。 たしかに‥‥
English: Huh!? Oh, oh yeah. That's right.

Japanese: でもさ。オヤジならアイサツしたら ムシなんてことはないし。
English: But then we said hello and didn't even get a reply.

Japanese: 例のイヤミな３点セットも ぶら下げていたし‥‥。
English: Not to mention he was draped in those gaudy symbols.

Japanese: ‥‥じゃあ、もし それがなかったら‥‥？
English: What would you have thought if he wasn't wearing those symbols?

Japanese: そうだなあ‥‥。 どう思うよ、ベン？
English: Hmm... What do you think Ben?

Japanese: う！　‥‥あ。あ、あれは、 そう、団長さんだったのでは‥‥
English: What!? Oh... Umm... I would have thought it was the Ringmaster...

Japanese: （ベンさんたちが見たのは、 やっぱり、団長だったんだ！）
English: (Hmm... Something just isn't adding up here. I wonder who they really saw?)

Japanese: あの。ミリカちゃんの コトだけど‥‥
English: I was hoping I could ask you about Regina...

Japanese: オレはホンキだぜ！ 今もこうして待ってるんだ。
English: I'm completely serious about her! That's why I'm waiting for her even now!

Japanese: へええ、そうなんだ。
English: Really? That's so sweet...

Japanese: でも、ミリカちゃんに会うのなら、 テントの中を探せば‥‥
English: But if you really wanted to see Regina, shouldn't you check out the tent?

Japanese: はああ。なーんもわかってねえな、 ネンネちゃんは。
English: Hah! You haven't got a clue about things, do you sweetheart?

Japanese: え。
English: Eh?

Japanese: こうやって待つのも、 コイのうちなんだよ。
English: Waiting like this is part of being in love.

Japanese: は、はあ。
English: How so?

Japanese: いいか！　“コイ”という コトバは、“乞い”から来ている！
English: If you had a clue, you would know that waiting is such sweet, wonderful torture.

Japanese: 相手のアイをのぞんで身をコガす ‥‥これが、恋のゴクイなんだよ！
English: When your body aches for your partner's love... That's one of the best parts!!

Japanese: へ、へええ‥‥ な、なるほどねえ。
English: Umm... Y-Yeah... I-I knew that...

Japanese: （‥‥真宵ちゃん、 真っ赤になってるぞ）
English: (Poor Maya... She's so red she looks like a vine ripe tomato!)

Japanese: あの。あの。 新ネタなんですか？　今の？
English: So how is this new routine working out?

Japanese: いいかテメエら。 ゼーッタイ、ヒミツだからな！
English: Will you two just take a chill pill already!? Our routines are secret!!

Japanese: 腹話術界をゆるがす、 レボリューションを起こすんだ！
English: We're going to take the ventriloquism world by storm! It'll be a real revolution!

Japanese: ‥‥わあ。楽しみだなあ！
English: That sounds incredible!

Japanese: ‥‥あ。これだけは 言っとくケドなあ。
English: But let me make one thing clear...

Japanese: あのヤローにハッパかけられて やってるわけじゃねえぜ。
English: We're not going to take on the world just because that jerk said we should...

Japanese: あのヤロー？
English: That jerk?

Japanese: マックス・ギャラクティカ。
English: Max Galactica.

Japanese: “芸人は世界を目ざせ！” なんて、エラそうに！
English: "Performers should aim for the world!" Who does he think he is!?

Japanese: お。リロくん、ヤル気だね！
English: Trilo, you seem to really be fired up about all this!!

Japanese: 見てろ。世界を取るのは、 テメエだけじゃねえんだ。
English: He needs to realize that he isn't the only one who can conquer the world stage!

Japanese: そうだそうだ！
English: You're right! You're right!

Japanese: このリロ様も、かならず つかむぜ！　グランプリを！
English: Mark my words... I, Trilo Quist, will win the Grand Prix!!

Japanese: つかむぞ！
English: You're the man now, doll!

Japanese: “かえるのうた”でな！
English: "Row-row-row-your-boat!" will be the key to a glorious victory!!

Japanese: あの。‥‥もうちょっと ワールドワイドな選曲のほうが。
English: Umm... Not to rain on your parade, but wouldn't a more mature song be best?

Japanese: まあまあ。これは大いなる ヤボウの第１歩だからな！
English: Hey... You've got to start somewhere right?

Japanese: しっかりしろよなー。 やるのはオマエなんだからさー。
English: Don't screw this up! You've gotta be a part of this too.

Japanese: あの、ちょっと 見ていただけますか？
English: Would you mind taking a look at this?

Japanese: う。‥‥お、あ‥‥ ボ、ボクはその。
English: Uh... Umm... Uh... I-I-I-I...

Japanese: え。うお。 そ、そういうアレじゃ‥‥
English: Umm... That... U-U-U-Uhh... I-I-I-I-It's...

Japanese: ‥‥やっぱり、いいです。
English: I guess we won't need you to look at this after all.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 タチミ・サーカス　テント内
English: December 29 Berry Big Circus Big Top

Japanese: あれ。ミリカちゃんは？
English: Huh? Where's Regina?

Japanese: さあね。
English: I dunno...

Japanese: また、トラでもけしかけられたら タマんないからね。
English: But if she's with that tiger, I don't want to find out!!

Japanese: 早くどこか行こう。
English: Let's hurry up and get out of here!

Japanese: わあ。なるほどくん、 コシヌケだなあ。
English: Hehe... Nick. You're kinda a chicken, aren't you?

Japanese: いやいやいや。 相手はトラだぞ。ナマの！
English: No no no no no... I'm just... Umm... Allergic to wild tigers!

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 タチミ・サーカス　テント内
English: December 29 Berry Big Circus Big Top

Japanese: グルルルルル‥‥
English: Grrrrrrrrrrrrrr...

Japanese: （う‥‥イヤな予感‥‥）
English: (I've got a bad feeling about this...)

Japanese: グルルルルル‥‥ ガオオオオォォォォッッッ！
English: Grrrrrrrrrrr... Grawwwwwww!!

Japanese: きゃああっ！　なるほどくうん！
English: YAAAH!! NICK!!

Japanese: （こここ、今度こそ食われる！）
English: (Wonderful... Today's special must be Filet O'Phoenix!)

Japanese: ステイ！　ラトー！　ステイ！
English: STAY! STAY! HEEL!

Japanese: なんだー、真宵ちゃんたちかあ。
English: Oh! Maya! Nick! It's you guys!

Japanese: ‥‥ごめんね。 まちがえちゃったみたい。
English: I'm sorry... I guess I made a mistake.

Japanese: まま、まちがえた？
English: A m-m-mistake!?

Japanese: うん。ちょっと。
English: Yeah. A little one.

Japanese: こらしめちゃおうかな、 って思ったんだけど‥‥
English: I was thinking of teaching whatever primate was out there a lesson...

Japanese: ヒトちがい！　っていうか‥‥ サルちがい、かな？
English: But I was expecting more of a monkey than a human...

Japanese: さる‥‥？
English: A monkey?

Japanese: 団長さんのこと ‥‥おキノドクだね。
English: It's a pity about what happened to the Ringmaster.

Japanese: パパ？
English: Dad?

Japanese: みんなから愛されてた、 ステキなヒトだったもんね。
English: Everyone loved him, didn't they? He must have been quite a man.

Japanese: そうだよ！　ミリカ、 パパのコト、だーい好き！
English: He was!! I love my dad sooo much!

Japanese: （そのワリには、なんか あまり悲しそうに見えない‥‥）
English: (I hate to say it, but she doesn't seem all that broken up about her father...)

Japanese: ‥‥だからね。ちょっぴり さみしいんだよ。
English: That's why I feel so lonely.

Japanese: しばらく会えないから‥‥パパ。
English: Now that I won't be able to see him for a while...

Japanese: “しばらく”‥‥？
English: For a while?

Japanese: ‥‥うん。
English: Yeah.

Japanese: レオンが死んじゃったとき、 パパから聞いたの。
English: When Léon died, I talked with my dad, and he told me that...

Japanese: “死んだら、みんな、お星さまに なるんだよ”‥‥って。
English: "When someone dies, they just become a star in the heavens."

Japanese: おほ‥‥おほしさま‥‥
English: A s-star?

Japanese: それで‥‥ミリカたちのコト、 お空から見ててくれるんだって。
English: That means that my dad is looking down on me from the sky.

Japanese: だからね。ミリカ、夜が好き。 ‥‥みんなに会えるから。
English: That's why I love the night so much. I can see everyone who's gone.

Japanese: （“死んだらお星さま” なんて‥‥）
English: ("When someone dies, they just become a star in the heavens." That's kinda sweet...)

Japanese: （さすがの真宵ちゃんでも 信じてないぞ‥‥）
English: (But I bet you there's no way that Maya believes that...)

Japanese: ナニよ“さすがの”って！
English: What do you mean "there's no way I believe that"!?

Japanese: ミリカも‥‥いつか、 お星さまになるんだよね？
English: Do you think that one day... I'll be a star too?

Japanese: え！
English: Of course!

Japanese: ‥‥そうなんだよね？
English: You really think so?

Japanese: う、うん。たぶん。
English: Yeah... You will. I think...

Japanese: パパたちね。きっと 元気にしてると思うんだ！
English: I've got a feeling that everyone is doing great up there in the sky!

Japanese: （い、いいのかなあ‥‥）
English: (I wonder if everything's alright with Regina...)

Japanese: さっきの話だけど‥‥ ルーサーをこらしめる、って？
English: Just to go back and clear something up... Why'd you want to teach Money a lesson?

Japanese: ひどいんだよ、ルーサー。
English: Because he's a meanie!

Japanese: ミリカのね。ダイジなものを ‥‥持ってっちゃったの。
English: He's got something that means a lot to me...

Japanese: ダイジなもの‥‥？ やっぱり、ピカピカなのかな。
English: Something that means a lot to you? It must be something shiny right?

Japanese: うん。あのね。舞台衣装。
English: Umm... Actually... It's a stage costume.

Japanese: スパンコールが入ってて、 すっごいキレイなんだよー。
English: It's got lots of spangles... It's really beautiful, you should see it.

Japanese: “すぱんこーる”‥‥って？
English: What are spangles?

Japanese: スポットライトを当てると、 キラキラ光るの。
English: When the costume gets hit by the spotlight, it dazzles.

Japanese: ねえ！　弁護士さん。
English: Hey Mr. Attorney!

Japanese: ん？
English: Huh?

Japanese: 弁護士さん、ルーサーを見たら 取りもどしてくれるよね？
English: If you saw that monkey, you'd get my dress back for me, wouldn't you?

Japanese: ミリカのダイジなもの。‥‥ね？
English: It's really important to me...

Japanese: まかせといて！　あたしたちに。
English: Leave it all up to us!

Japanese: （‥‥ホント、 ことわれないんだよな‥‥）
English: (There's no way I could turn down that request...)

Japanese: やったあ！　きっとよ？
English: Yay! You're really gonna do it!?

Japanese: レオン‥‥て、たしか 殺されちゃった‥‥んだよね？
English: Léon... He was killed, wasn't he?

Japanese: そうなの。パパにね。
English: That's right. My dad killed him.

Japanese: ねえ！　どうして！
English: Why!?

Japanese: あのね。芸の練習で‥‥ シッパイしちゃったの。
English: Well... Léon did something really bad during practice.
During practice?

Japanese: レオンが“おすわり”してね、 お口を開けるの。ガーッて。
English: Léon was sitting down, and then he opened his mouth. You know... GAAAWWW...

Japanese: うんうん。
English: Uh huh. Uh huh.

Japanese: でね。ミリカが、その中に あたまを入れるの。
English: Usually when he did that, I would put my head into his mouth.

Japanese: うんうん。‥‥えええええッ！ ララ、ライオンの口の中に？
English: Uh huh. Uh huh... Wait... You put your HEAD into a lion's MOUTH!?

Japanese: そうだよ。
English: I sure did.

Japanese: そうすると、お客さんが 悲鳴を上げてよろこんでくれるの。
English: The people in the crowd always loved seeing me do that! They'd always start screaming!

Japanese: （よろこんでるのか？ それって‥‥）
English: (You sure they were screaming because they loved seeing you do that!?)

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥！ まさか‥‥“シッパイ”って！
English: Anyways... What was the "bad thing"?

Japanese: そうなの。 レオン‥‥かみついちゃったの。
English: Oh yeah. Léon bit someone during that practice.

Japanese: みみ、ミリカちゃん、 よくブジだったね‥‥。
English: R-R-Regina!! Everything was alright though right?

Japanese: ブジじゃなかったけど‥‥。 パパ、すごく怒ったの。
English: No, it wasn't alright... That was the problem. My dad was incredibly angry.

Japanese: ‥‥それで、レオンは‥‥
English: And that's when Léon...

Japanese: うん。 ‥‥お星さまになっちゃった。
English: Yeah. That's when he became a star in the sky.

Japanese: なんてこった‥‥
English: Poor thing...

Japanese: 《レオン》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Léon has been added to the Court Record.

Japanese: これなんだけど‥‥
English: Take a look at this...

Japanese: だ、ダメだよなるほどくん！ そんな写真見せちゃあ。
English: NICK! YOU CAN'T SHOW HER THAT PICTURE!!

Japanese: あ‥‥、そうだね。
English: Ouch... Yeah, that would be a bit insensitive of me.

Japanese: なあに？ ミリカにも見せてよー。
English: Huh? Show me! Show me! Show me!

Japanese: ‥‥やっぱりマックス、 カッコイイよねー。
English: There's no denying it... Max is cool.

Japanese: ミリカも飛んでみたいな。 マックスと。
English: I wanna try flying someday! With Max!!

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥すっかり ユメの世界にひたってるよ‥‥）
English: (She's already flown off into her own little world...)

Japanese: なるほどくん。 あたしも飛んでみたい。
English: Nick, I wanna try flying too!

Japanese: うーん。 ‥‥考えておくよ。
English: Uhh... OK. I'll think about it.

Japanese: ホント、いたずらばっかり。 カワイイんだけどね。
English: He just loves to cause nothing but trouble... That cute little monkey.

Japanese: でも‥‥さすがに、トラを けしかけるのはちょっと‥‥。
English: But don't you think using a tiger to scare him is a bit much?

Japanese: え？　どうして。
English: Huh? Why's that?

Japanese: どうして、って‥‥。 アブナイじゃない！
English: Do you really have to ask that!? It's dangerous to use a tiger for that kinda thing!!

Japanese: だいじょうぶだよ。 ラトーはやさしいもん。
English: It's not dangerous at all. Regent's just a little kitten!

Japanese: （そういうコトじゃ ないんだけどなあ‥‥）
English: (I've seen plenty of kittens... And that's no kitten.)

Japanese: あ。それ、コショウでしょ？ 食堂にあった‥‥
English: That's pepper, isn't it? From the cafeteria...

Japanese: え、食堂‥‥？
English: Huh? It's from the cafeteria?

Japanese: ちがうのかな？ 見おぼえがあるけど。
English: It's not? I remember seeing it there.

Japanese: （食堂のコショウ だったのか‥‥）
English: (Hmm... So this was the cafeteria's pepper...)

Japanese: ミリカ、ちょっと ニガテなの。コショウ。
English: You know, I'm not a big fan of pepper.

Japanese: ねえ、ミリカちゃん。 これ、見たことある？
English: Regina, have you ever seen this before?

Japanese: あ‥‥。 うん、知ってるよ。
English: Uhh... I know what this is.

Japanese: ホ、ホント！
English: R-Really!?

Japanese: なんかね。ミリカのポッケにね、 いつの間にか入ってたの。
English: Well, it was in my pocket for a while.

Japanese: な‥‥なんだって！ このメモが‥‥ポケットに？
English: I-It was in your pocket!? This piece of paper... Was in your pocket!?

Japanese: あれかな。気がついたのは、 朝ゴハン持って行ったとき。
English: Hmm... I guess I noticed it was in there around breakfast time.

Japanese: 朝ゴハン‥‥？
English: Breakfast time?

Japanese: うん。アクロのゴハン、 いつもミリカが持って行くんだよ。
English: Yeah. I always take Acro his breakfast in the morning.

Japanese: ついでにね。お部屋の ゴミも捨ててあげるの。ミリカが。
English: That's when I also take out the trash in his room.

Japanese: それで、食堂に帰ってきて、 ミリカもゴハン食べようって‥‥。
English: Then I'd go to the cafeteria and eat my own breakfast...

Japanese: そのとき、メモに気がついたの？
English: That's when you realized the piece of paper was in your pocket?

Japanese: うん。でも‥‥ミリカは “さつじんしゃ”じゃないから、
English: Yep. But since I'm not a murderer,

Japanese: きっと、だれかのポッケと まちがえたんだと思うの。
English: I just figured it belonged in someone else's pocket.

Japanese: ‥‥それで？
English: And then what...?

Japanese: 落としたヒト、困ってるだろうな、 って思ったから‥‥。
English: I wondered if the person who lost it was in trouble, so...

Japanese: もしかして‥‥ 食堂の伝言板に？
English: You didn't put it on the bulletin board in the cafeteria, did you?

Japanese: そう！　張っておいたの。 ‥‥よく知ってるね？
English: I did! I stuck it up there. How'd you know...?

Japanese: は、ははは‥‥。 （ミリカちゃんが張ったのか！）
English: Hmm... (So it was Regina who put it up there!)

Japanese: それって‥‥いつのことかな？
English: When did this happen?

Japanese: あのね。 事件があった日の朝、かな。
English: Umm... The morning of the murder. I think.

Japanese: （‥‥やっぱり‥‥）
English: (That explains a lot...)

Japanese: だれが書いたんだろうね。 こんなの‥‥。
English: I wonder who wrote this...

Japanese: 証拠品《張り紙》のデータを 法廷記録に書きなおした。
English: Note updated in the Court Record.

Japanese: ‥‥なんだろうね。 “さつじんしゃ”なんて。
English: I wonder what they mean by murderer...

Japanese: テレビのドラマみたい。 ちょっと、スリルあるよね。
English: It's like some sort of soap opera. It's kinda strange.

Japanese: （スリルではすまない 気がするけど‥‥）
English: (Strange is probably too nice of a word...)

Japanese: ミリカちゃん！　はい、これ。
English: Here you go Regina!!

Japanese: わあ、ありがとう！ ホントに取りもどしてくれたんだ。
English: YAY! THANK YOU!! YOU REALLY GOT IT BACK FOR ME!!

Japanese: いやいや。
English: Don't mention it.

Japanese: 弁護士さん、だいすき！
English: I love you, Mr. Attorney!

Japanese: い‥‥いやその。
English: *blush* It's... Nothing...

Japanese: （こういうストレートさに、 オトコはヨワいのかな‥‥？）
English: (No wonder guys melt to mush in front of this girl...)

Japanese: ね。ね。ミリカちゃん。
English: Hey Regina.

Japanese: その衣装、ミリカちゃんの？ あたし、着られなかったよ。
English: That costume is yours right? I tried it on, but it didn't fit me...

Japanese: ‥‥これ？　ふふ。ちがうよ。 レオンの衣装。
English: Huh? This costume? This isn't mine. It was Léon's.

Japanese: レオン‥‥？
English: Léon's?

Japanese: ああ‥‥ホラ。ライオンの。
English: You know... The lion she told us about.

Japanese: （‥‥あれ？　たしか、 だれかに殺された、とか‥‥）
English: (Ohh... The one that someone killed.)

Japanese: これなんだけど、 どうかな？
English: What about this? What can you tell me?

Japanese: うーん。ミリカ、 むずかしいことはニガテだな。
English: Umm... I'm not really good at figuring out hard things.

Japanese: あ、ミリカちゃんも？ ‥‥話せるねえ。
English: Really? You too? But you're such a good talker...

Japanese: （ヘンなところで ウマが合ってしまったみたいだ）
English: (I never expected Maya to make a new friend in a strange place like this...)

Japanese: パパとね。ずいぶん ムカシからの友だちなの。
English: He's been a good friend to my dad for a really long time now.

Japanese: パパ、いつも言ってたよ。
English: My dad always said...

Japanese: 『サーカスのこと、まかせ られるのはアイツだけだ』って。
English: "When it comes to who I can entrust the circus to, it's definitely Moe."

Japanese: ふううん。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: そうだよね。すごく 楽しかったもん、あのピエロさん。
English: Yep! That clown is one lovable stooge.

Japanese: ね。なるほどくん！
English: Don't you think so, Nick?

Japanese: う‥‥うん。
English: Umm... No comment...

Japanese: わあ、よかった。 かわいく撮れてて。
English: Alright! I look so cute in this picture!

Japanese: かわいいでしょ？　ミリカ。 ‥‥ね？
English: Don't you think I look cute? Don't ya? Don't ya?

Japanese: カワイイよ！
English: You sure do!

Japanese: カワイイね！
English: No objections here!

Japanese: あ、そのヒト。 ベンさん、だっけ？
English: Ahh, that guy. His name is Ben, right?

Japanese: あれ。知らないの？
English: Huh? You don't know him?

Japanese: ううん‥‥。あんまり 目立たないから。
English: I dunno... He didn't really catch my eye.

Japanese: リロくんのお友だち なんだよね？
English: He's friends with Trilo right?

Japanese: （ぼくたちに 聞かれてもなあ‥‥）
English: (Wait... We're the ones asking the questions around here...)

Japanese: “霊媒師”だっけ？ 真宵ちゃん。
English: Maya... You're a spirit channeler?

Japanese: うん！ まだ修行中なんだけどね。
English: Yep! I'm still training though.

Japanese: 楽しそうだよねえ。
English: That sounds like fun!

Japanese: そうだ！　ミリカたちと いっしょにやろうよ！
English: I think you should join us at the circus!

Japanese: え。‥‥霊媒を？
English: Huh? As a spirit channeler?

Japanese: きっとね。 サーカスの人気者になるよ！
English: Yeah! You'd be a big hit!

Japanese: ホント！ ねえ、ねえ。人気者だって！
English: Really!? She says I'd be a big hit!

Japanese: （なんてヘンジすれば いいんだよ‥‥？）
English: (I think she's just being nice.)

Japanese: あ。ムチ持ってるんだ このヒト。
English: Cool! This lady's got a whip!

Japanese: やっぱり、猛獣使いなの？
English: She's an animal tamer, huh?

Japanese: うーん‥‥どうなのかな。
English: Hmm... Sort of...

Japanese: （使われてるの、ぼくとか おとなしいジイさんだけど）
English: (Two women with whips... That's my cue to shut up and act like a good boy...)

Japanese: ねえ、真宵ちゃん。 マックス、元気かなあ？
English: Hey Maya, do you know if Max is doing OK?

Japanese: え！　うーん‥‥ ゲンキ‥‥とは言えないかなあ。
English: Well... Umm... I don't think I'd go so far as to say he was OK...

Japanese: ふふ。早く帰ってこないかな、 マックス‥‥。
English: Aww... I hope he comes back here real soon.

Japanese: 帰ってくる、って信じきってるよ ‥‥なるほどくん。
English: I'm sure he's going to come back here... Right, Nick?

Japanese: だって、助けてくれるんでしょ？ 弁護士さん。‥‥ね？
English: There's no way he will lose with you helping him... Right, Mr. Attorney?

Japanese: う、うん。 モチロンだよ‥‥。
English: Umm... *gulp* Of course. If I'm Wright, you're right!!

Japanese: パパとはしばらく 会えないけど、
English: I might not be able to see my Dad for a while, but...

Japanese: ミリカ、ガマンするから。
English: I know I can wait until I see him again.

Japanese: そ、そうだね。 もう‥‥帰ってこないんだから。
English: That's the spirit. Even though he won't be coming home...

Japanese: もう、ダメだよー。 真宵ちゃんがそんなカオしちゃ。
English: Don't do that Maya. Please don't look at me that way.

Japanese: ごご、‥‥ごめんね。
English: I-I'm s-s-sorry.

Japanese: （‥‥どうも、このミリカちゃんの 明るさが気になる‥‥）
English: (Regina's so cheerful... It kinda makes you wonder if all the lights are on...)

Japanese: （“しばらく”会えないという 表現も‥‥）
English: (Especially when she says she won't be able to see her dad "for a while"...)

Japanese: ‥‥あ。アクロだ。 今日は部屋にいるの？
English: Ahh, it's Acro. Is he in his room today?

Japanese: う、うん。 さっき、会ってきたよ。
English: Yes, he is. We just came back from meeting with him.

Japanese: 早くよくなるといいよねー、足。
English: I hope his legs get better soon.

Japanese: アクロ、スゴいんだよ！ ブランコなんか、特にね！
English: Acro's so incredible! Especially on the trapeze!

Japanese: ブランコって、あの てんじょうからブラ下がってる？
English: The trapeze is that enormous swing hanging from the top of the tent, right?

Japanese: うん。それ。
English: Yep, that's it!

Japanese: ああ、また見たいなー。 ‥‥アクロのブランコ。
English: I really want to see him up there again... Acro the Acrobat.

Japanese: （おかしいな‥‥）
English: (That's strange...)

Japanese: （アクロさん、ミリカちゃんに 悪意があるみたいだったけど）
English: (Acro doesn't seem to have very many nice things to say about Regina...)

Japanese: （ミリカちゃんのほうに、 そんなケハイは‥‥ない）
English: (But Regina seems to like Acro just fine.)

Japanese: この人なんだけど‥‥。
English: What do you think about this person?

Japanese: ごめんね。
English: Sorry...
Sorry?

Japanese: ミリカ、もうココロに 決めたヒトがいるから。
English: I've already given my heart to another man.

Japanese: その人とは、おつきあい できないなあ。
English: So I'm not interested in enrolling in your dating service.

Japanese: そ‥‥それはザンネン。
English: Huh? I just wanted to know if you knew this person.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 テント内　食堂
English: December 29 Big Top Cafeteria

Japanese: まだ、かたづけてないよ‥‥。
English: They still haven't cleaned this place up yet.

Japanese: はみちゃんが このアリサマを見たら、
English: If Pearly got one look at the state of this place...

Japanese: ひっぱたいちゃうよ！ ‥‥セキニンシャ。
English: She'd slap whoever was in charge across the face!!

Japanese: （‥‥うちの事務所には 呼ばないようにしよう‥‥）
English: (Remind me to never invite her to my office.)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 テント内　食堂
English: December 29 Big Top Cafeteria

Japanese: お、ニイちゃん！ ようこそ、わが食堂へ！
English: Ah! Wright!! Welcome to the wonderful, the fabulous, the cafeteria!

Japanese: （うわあ‥‥ あいかわらず絶好調だな）
English: (Yikes... He's in an awfully good mood...)

Japanese: さあ！　ここで コンテンポラリ・クイズでーす！
English: Alright! You know what time it is!! Riddle time!!

Japanese: イノウエさんちは店をやってます。 さあて！　なんの店でしょーかッ？
English: Why does everyone cry when they eat Mexican pizza?
Umm...

Japanese: な・ん・の・お店でしょーかッ！
English: C'mon! You can answer this! It's easy!!

Japanese: ‥‥イノウエ商店。
English: ...Because cafeteria Mexican pizza is possibly a weapon of bowel destruction?

Japanese: ブブーッ！ ダメだよマジメに考えなくちゃあ！
English: *BUZZ* Wrong! Try again!

Japanese: ハイ、おぢょうさんは？
English: OK! What do you think, girlie?

Japanese: んーとね。 ‥‥あッ！　わかった！
English: Umm... Ahh! I got it!!

Japanese: おっ！　さあ、コタエはっ！
English: OK!! What is your answer?

Japanese: 食堂！
English: Because they are in the Café Teary Eye!

Japanese: ぴんぽんぴんぽん！　“胃の上” だから“食道”‥‥なーんて。
English: Exactly!! It's an incredibly sad place, that café.

Japanese: あひゃ。あひゃひゃあひゃあひゃ。 あひゃっひゃ。ひゃひゃあひゃ！
English: Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha!

Japanese: やったぁ！
English: I did it!!

Japanese: （なぜだ‥‥？ ヒジョーにくやしい‥‥）
English: (What's going on? He's being too nice...)

Japanese: 今日は、タイヘンなことに なっちゃいましたね。
English: Today's been a really crazy day, hasn't it?

Japanese: まったくサ。あんなに重いとは 思わなかったよ。
English: You're telling me! I didn't think it was going to be so tough.
Tough?

Japanese: 客だよ。この世界じゃア、 反応が悪い客のコトをそう言うの。
English: Yeah. It was a tough crowd. That's what you call a crowd that refuses to laugh.

Japanese: “今日の客、重いな”って。
English: For instance, it was such a tough crowd last night I had to smash watermelons.

Japanese: はあ。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: とびっきりの小話をしたのに、 ノッてきやしねえ。
English: I told them all a great story and even greater jokes, but no one busted out laughing...

Japanese: “パンツが破けた” ってヤツですか？
English: You even used the famed "No shoes, no shirt, no service" joke!

Japanese: それそれ！　‥‥ッたく、 どういうセンスしてるんだよ！
English: Exactly! How can you not laugh at stunning comedy like that!?

Japanese: 今日の証言ですけど‥‥ やっぱり、まちがいないんですか？
English: Are you 100% sure about your testimony today?

Japanese: オレっちは見たんだ！ あのヤローが、フワリとこう‥‥
English: I saw what I saw! I swear! That creep just...

Japanese: 空を飛んだ‥‥？
English: Flew through the air?

Japanese: 飛んだっていうか‥‥ まあ、浮いたわけだよ。
English: It wasn't exactly flying per say... It was more like floating.

Japanese: ‥‥カオのシルエットも ゼッタイ、マックスだったんだ！
English: The silhouette of his face made me positive it was Max!

Japanese: （サイコ・ロックも見えないし ‥‥ウソはついてない、か‥‥）
English: (I don't see a Psyche-Lock... He must be telling the truth.)

Japanese: ‥‥アイツの像なら、そこの小さな テーブルにかざってあったよ。
English: Max's bust should be on that small table over there.

Japanese: ‥‥何もありませんよ。
English: There's nothing over there.

Japanese: ああ‥‥そういえば。
English: Really? Oh yeah.

Japanese: いつだったかなあ。 もう５日ぐらいたつかなあ。
English: Hmm... When was it...? I'd say about five days ago, all of a sudden...

Japanese: 急に、なくなっちゃってさあ。
English: The bust disappeared.

Japanese: なくなった‥‥？
English: It disappeared?

Japanese: 写真でよけりゃ、そこの 伝言板に張ってあるぜ。
English: If you want to see it, there's a photo on the bulletin board over there.

Japanese: マックスのヤツ、そこら中で 写真、くばってたから。
English: Max just had to put the picture up.

Japanese: ひゃあ‥‥こりゃ カッコイイなあ‥‥！
English: Hmm... Hey! This thing is really cool!

Japanese: ね。ね。なるほどくん！ あたしも、胸像‥‥
English: Nick! Nick! I want someone to make a bust of me!

Japanese: 買ってやらないぞ。
English: Sure, as long as I'm not paying for it.

Japanese: ううう‥‥。
English: Aww...

Japanese: 証拠品《マックスの胸像》の 写真を法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Max G. Bust added to the Court Record.

Japanese: 他には、何か変わったこと、 ありませんでしたか？
English: Is there anything else that's changed about this place?

Japanese: 変わったこと、ねえ。 うーん‥‥。
English: Nope. Nothing's changed that I can see...

Japanese: （思い出してるのか、それとも ギャグを考えてるのか‥‥？）
English: (I wonder if he's actually thinking about this, or if he is setting up a bad joke?)

Japanese: ‥‥ダメだ。 ナニも浮かばないや。
English: Nope nope... I'm drawing a blank here.

Japanese: （‥‥ギャグのほうだった）
English: (A quiet Moe is a good Moe in my book.)

Japanese: やっぱり、ないですか ‥‥変わったコト。
English: I guess there really aren't any other things that have changed, huh?

Japanese: ん？　いやいや。 ‥‥それならあったよ。
English: Well... There is this one tinsy tiny thing that does seem different.

Japanese: 聞かせてください！
English: Tell us! Tell us!

Japanese: ‥‥いやサ。事件の朝、そこの 伝言板にさあ‥‥、
English: Well... On the morning of the crime, over on that bulletin board...

Japanese: こんなものが張って あったのよ。
English: This piece of paper was posted front and center.

Japanese: メモ‥‥ですか？
English: Piece of paper?

Japanese: 破けてて本文は読めないけど、 タイトルがなあ‥‥。
English: It's torn, so I don't know what it said, but I could see it's title.

Japanese: うわっ！ “殺人者に告ぐ”‥‥だって！
English: Yikes! It says, "To The Murderer!!"

Japanese: さ‥‥さつじんしゃ‥‥！
English: M-M-M-Murderer!?

Japanese: ダレが張ったのか‥‥ そして、ダレが破いたのか？
English: Yup, that's what it says. But the rest of it has been ripped off.

Japanese: オレっち、知らないけどさ。
English: And I don't know who posted it.

Japanese: ‥‥あの。 これを見つけたのは‥‥？
English: Umm... When did you find this?

Japanese: 事件の日の朝だよ。
English: The morning before the murder.

Japanese: 事件の日の‥‥？
English: Before the murder!?

Japanese: そう。団長は、この張り紙の あった夜‥‥亡くなったんだ。
English: Yes. The Ringmaster was killed the night after this paper was discovered.

Japanese: （いったい‥‥ だれが張ったんだろう‥‥？）
English: (Who in the world posted this thing...?)

Japanese: 証拠品《張り紙》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Note added to the Court Record.

Japanese: なるほどくん！　これは ちゃんと調べたほうがいいね！
English: Nick! I think this is a very important clue!

Japanese: おッ、その写真！ ニイちゃんたちも見せられたか！
English: Ahh! Not this picture!! He showed it to you guys too?

Japanese: え‥‥？ じゃあ、トミーさんも？
English: Huh? You've seen it as well?

Japanese: ‥‥ホント、あのハナシを してるときのアイツこそ、
English: Well, you know what they say about Maximillion Galactica...

Japanese: まさに“向かうところ手品師” ‥‥なーんて！
English: He really gets around.

Japanese: あひゃ。ひゃひゃひゃあひゃ。 ひゃひゃひゃあひゃあひゃ！
English: Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha!

Japanese: うまいッ！
English: Hehe.

Japanese: あ。それに‥‥
English: Oh yeah...

Japanese: 写真だけじゃないぜ。 見せられたの。
English: He didn't just show me the picture!

Japanese: どういうことですか？
English: What do you mean?

Japanese: 胸像だよキョウゾウ。 等身大の、でっかいヤツ。
English: He showed me his bust too. Let me tell you, that thing is enormous!

Japanese: 写真にも写ってるだろ？
English: It's in the picture, I think.

Japanese: 毎日、アレを おがまされるワケよ。オレたち。
English: He'd make us worship it... Everyday. He made us bow to his greatness.

Japanese: （等身大の‥‥胸像か）
English: (He's got a... Big... Bust?)

Japanese: （もう少し聞きたいな ‥‥そのハナシ）
English: (I wouldn't mind hearing more about Max's bust... Not that I'm into that sorta thing...)

Japanese: ‥‥オレたちゃ、マックスの 引き立て役なんてマッピラだぜ！
English: I can't stand Max and his attitude sometimes!

Japanese: そ、そうですよね‥‥
English: I know...

Japanese: でも、最近‥‥
English: But lately...

Japanese: え？
English: Huh?

Japanese: イヤ。アイツの言うことも、 わかる気がしてきたんだよ。
English: It's just... Lately I've started to understand what he is always going on about.

Japanese: そ、それって‥‥？
English: What is it then?

Japanese: イヤ、オレっちにもまだ、 うまく言えないんだけどサ。
English: Well... Umm... I'm not sure if I can explain it all that well just yet...

Japanese: （トミーさん‥‥ いつになく、マジメだな‥‥）
English: (Just when you think it's impossible, Moe flashes a glimpse of maturity...)

Japanese: ‥‥団長！
English: RUSSELL!!

Japanese: こんなことに なっちまって‥‥
English: How could anyone do this to you...

Japanese: オレは‥‥オレはぁぁぁ！ I... I...
English: *sobs*

Japanese: トミーさん‥‥ かわいそう。
English: Moe's taking this really hard...

Japanese: 事件のあった朝、伝言板に 張ってあったんだ。
English: The morning of the murder, this was posted up on the bulletin board.

Japanese: あのときは、マックスがベンを 殴ったりしてさ。
English: That's around when Max clonked Ben over the head.

Japanese: ちょっと、今まで 忘れてたんだケドさ。
English: Sorry I hadn't remembered it until now.

Japanese: ‥‥いつのまにか 破かれてたんだよ。
English: It was torn down before anyone really paid any attention to it.

Japanese: ナニが書いてあったか、 知らないんですか？
English: So you don't know what was written on it, do you?

Japanese: うーん。ザンネンだけど。
English: Unfortunately I don't.

Japanese: ‥‥あ。もしかしたら、 あいつらなら知ってるかもな。
English: But I betcha they would know!

Japanese: あいつら‥‥？
English: Who are "they"?

Japanese: マックスとベンだよ。
English: Max and Ben.

Japanese: あの２人、オレっちより先に 食堂でメシ食ってたから。
English: They were at the cafeteria earlier than I was that morning.

Japanese: ワルいけど、特に 心当たり、ないなあ。
English: Sorry, but I don't know anything about that.

Japanese: そうですか‥‥。
English: I see...

Japanese: ホラ。オレっち、ピエロだし。
English: After all, I'm just a clown.

Japanese: （‥‥それは カンケイないだろ‥‥）
English: (I concede Moe's a clown, but I get the feeling he's not your average clown...)

Japanese: こいつとは、サーカスを 始める前からのつきあいだよ。
English: I've been friends with Russell since even before the circus began.

Japanese: 自分のことより、いっつも なかまのことばかり考えていた。
English: He was so selfless. He always thought about others before thinking of himself.

Japanese: 恩を返すつもりがさ。 いつも世話になりっぱなし。
English: I'll find a way to return the favor... He always took such good care of me.

Japanese: ヒトコト“ありがとう”って 言っておけばよかったよ‥‥。
English: I wish I would have thanked him more than I did...

Japanese: 団長のムスメさんだ。
English: That's the Ringmaster's daughter, Regina.

Japanese: 生まれたころから ずーっと見てきてる。
English: Ever since she was a little girl, she's been watching the circus performances.

Japanese: カワイイよなあ。 ウチのトモコとちがってさあ。
English: Cuter than a little puppy Maltese, that Regina. If only my Larina was that cute...

Japanese: トモコちゃん？
English: Larina?

Japanese: いや、オレっちのムスメ。
English: Ah... Larina is my daughter.

Japanese: かあちゃんといっしょに 出て行っちまったけど。
English: She lives with her mother now.

Japanese: （さすがピエロ‥‥。 ホロ苦い人生を歩んでるな）
English: (It's true what they say about the tears of a clown...)

Japanese: お。ベンちゃん。どうかした？
English: This is Ben. Is something wrong?

Japanese: 腹話術師なんですよね？
English: He's a ventriloquist, right?

Japanese: そうよ。‥‥なんたって、
English: That's correct.

Japanese: ヘソのかわりに口がついてるん だから、ビックリ。‥‥なーんて。
English: Boy was I surprised to find out he's got a mouth for a belly button!

Japanese: あひゃ。ひゃひゃひゃあひゃ。 ひゃひゃひゃあひゃあひゃ！
English: Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha!
Ah!

Japanese: どうしたんですか？
English: What's the matter?

Japanese: なんか、自分のカオ見ると、 ギャグが出て来なくなるんだ。
English: Whenever I see my own nose, I am reminded of my Italian heritage...

Japanese: ははあ。
English: Ahhhh... I see...

Japanese: それってやっぱり、“シャレに ならないカオ”ってコトですか？
English: It must be because you have one of those "Roman Noses"... Roamin' all over your face.

Japanese: なな、なんかマズいコト 言っちゃったかな？
English: I didn't cross the line there did I?

Japanese: 言っちゃったんじゃないか？ やっぱり。
English: You went so far past it, you'll need a map to get back!

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 テント内　食堂
English: December 29 Big Top Cafeteria

Japanese: ‥‥ありゃ。 いなくなっちゃった、トミーさん。
English: Wha...? Moe's gone.

Japanese: 伝言板に メッセージが書いてあるぞ。
English: There's a message on the bulletin board.

Japanese: 『ハラへったから、ラーメン 買ってきます‥‥トミー』
English: "I'm hungry so I'm off to get some hamburgers! Love, Moe."

Japanese: ら、らーめん‥‥。
English: Mmmm... Hammburrgeerrrr...

Japanese: そういえば、
English: Just thinking about it is making me hungry!

Japanese: なんだか急に、おなか すいてきたねえ、なるほどくん。
English: All of a sudden I need a burger bad!!

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥）
English: (All of a sudden, I need a new partner... BAD!!)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 テント内　食堂
English: December 29 Big Top Cafeteria

Japanese: ‥‥うわ、いいニオイ！
English: Something smells fantastic!

Japanese: なるほどくん！ みそラーメンのニオイだよ！
English: So we know it can't be Moe... Wait! I know what it is!! It's burgers!!

Japanese: おッ！　ようこそわが食堂へ！
English: Hello hello hello! Welcome to the Bistro du Cirque aka the cafeteria!

Japanese: お、おいしそうですね ‥‥みそラーメン。
English: Mmm... It smells so good in here... Those burgers look great!

Japanese: （ココロなしか、 声がうわずってるぞ‥‥）
English: (She's drooling like she' some sort of crazed burger monster!)

Japanese: うまいよー、ラーメン。
English: My burgers are the best!!

Japanese: ちぢれたメンが、コクのある スープと連れそって、
English: Juicy meat... Toasted buns... Special sauce...

Japanese: ノドをするりと通りすぎてゆく、 この瞬間。
English: They are absolutely irresistible to anyone with a hankering for a burger!

Japanese: 生きてて一番の シアワセだねえ。
English: One bite will send you into hamburger heaven!

Japanese: ホント、そうですよねー。
English: I bet! I can tell by the smell.

Japanese: （すっかり仲よくなってしまった。 スゴいな‥‥ラーメンのパワー）
English: (Whoa... I'm getting hungry too! Those burgers must have some kind of special power!)

Japanese: ‥‥そんなコトが あったなんて‥‥
English: So that's what really happened...

Japanese: まあまあまあ！　アンタたちが シンミリしてどうするんだよ！
English: Well... You guys were so serious! What was I supposed to do!? I had to tell you!

Japanese: ホラ。オレっちも、 ラーメン食っちゃうからね！
English: But all this truthfulness has put me in the mood for a burger!

Japanese: ‥‥ちょっと、コショウなんか ふったりして。パッパッパッと！
English: Here... You two have some pepper! *shaka shaka shaka shaka*

Japanese: （うわ‥‥また、 ハデにぶちまけたな‥‥）
English: (There he goes again, acting like his normal crazy self...)
Ahh... Ahh... Ahh...

Japanese: ハーックショ、ハックショ！
English: *Achoo!* *Achoo!*

Japanese: お。いいねいいね！ そのクシャミ。
English: Nice! What a wonderful sneeze!!

Japanese: え。そ、そうですか？
English: Huh? You think so?

Japanese: コショウでクシャミ。バナナで コケる。‥‥これ、キホンね。
English: You sneeze with pepper and slip on a banana... That's basic clownsmanship.

Japanese: おぢょーさん、 素質がアル・カポネ。なーんて。
English: Girlie, I know you "Gotti" understand that!!

Japanese: なるほどくん！ あたし、イイかも！　ピエロ！
English: Nick! I think I'd make a good clown!

Japanese: ここまでミゴトなクシャミは、 ミリカ以来だな。
English: Other than Regina, I've never seen a cuter sneezer!

Japanese: へええ。 ミリカちゃんも、コショウで‥‥？
English: Heh heh! Does Regina sneeze with pepper too?

Japanese: ああ。よく、イタズラされてたよ。 ‥‥バットのヤツにさ。
English: She does! Bat would always tease her with pepper!

Japanese: ば、バットさん。
English: B-Bat?

Japanese: オレっちから見ても、おニアイ だったのに‥‥あのふたり。
English: From my point of view, those two always looked so perfect together...

Japanese: そうだったんですか‥‥。
English: They looked perfect together, huh?

Japanese: 団長さんがいなくなって‥‥ これから、どうするんですか？
English: Now that the Ringmaster is gone, what are you going to do?

Japanese: それ！　オレっちも、 ずっと考えてたんだ‥‥。
English: That's all I've thought about the past two days...

Japanese: みんな、団長が大好きだった。 ‥‥アクロの話、聞いたかい？
English: Everyone loved Russell... You've heard Acro's story haven't you?

Japanese: 小さいころ、引き取られた ‥‥とか。
English: Like how he was adopted when he was younger?

Japanese: 今はなんとか落ちついたけど、 事件があったときはスゴかった。
English: He's calmed down a bit now, but when the murder happened he was livid.

Japanese: あとを追って自殺しかねない イキオイだったんだぜ。
English: Acro was so upset that he said he couldn't go on.

Japanese: そ‥‥そうだったんですか‥‥
English: He was that upset?

Japanese: とにかくさ。考えたんだよ。
English: Yes he was... Anyways, I gave it some thought.

Japanese: オレっち、ウケないピエロ なんてやめて‥‥その。
English: Maybe I should give up on trying to be a half-baked clown...

Japanese: 団長になろうかな‥‥なんてさ。
English: I've been thinking of trying on the Ringmaster's shoes...

Japanese: えっ！　そうなんですか！
English: What!? Really!?

Japanese: ああ。今なら‥‥アイツ。 マックスもいる。
English: Max would still be an issue though...

Japanese: マックス‥‥？
English: Max?

Japanese: 口がワルすぎるけど‥‥ヤッパリ、 言ってるコトは、その‥‥なんだ。
English: He may be a bit mean and hard to work with, but it's hard to argue his importance.

Japanese: タダシイ‥‥気がするからサ。
English: He's probably the reason the circus is still around... A lot of what he says is right.

Japanese: トミーさん‥‥。
English: Moe...

Japanese: あとは、みんなが 乗りこえられるかどうかだな。
English: All that's left is to see if everyone would get over the tragedy...

Japanese: “あのコト”を‥‥。
English: You know...

Japanese: （‥‥なんだ？ “あのコト”‥‥って）
English: (The tragedy? You know? What is he talking about?)

Japanese: “あのコト”を乗りこえる‥‥ どういうことですか？
English: "Get over" what tragedy, Moe?

Japanese: ‥‥え！　あいやいやいやいや。 べべ、ベツに気にしないで！
English: Huh!? Don't worry about it... It's nothing. Nothing at all.

Japanese: きっと、団長さんの死を 乗りこえる、ってコトですよね？
English: He must mean the tragic death of the Ringmaster, right?

Japanese: お！　おぢょーさん、それ！
English: Yes Yes! That's it! You're right, girlie!

Japanese: ビンボ！　ビンボ！
English: Dang! Dang! Dang! Correct-o-mundo!!

Japanese: （“びんご”だろ‥‥）
English: (Doesn't he mean "ding"?)

Japanese: ‥‥トミーさん。 ウソをついてませんか？
English: Moe... I mean no disrespect here, but... Are you lying to us?

Japanese: えげェッ！　どして！ どーしてなのだ！
English: Egghhh... No! Not at all! What makes you think that!?

Japanese: 「“あのコト”を乗りこえる」 という表現は‥‥
English: Just the way you said "if everyone would get over it" seemed a bit strange.

Japanese: ‥‥もっと、ムカシのことを 言ってるように聞こえるからです。
English: It sounded like you were talking about something from a long time ago.

Japanese: ‥‥グギギギギギギギギ。
English: Eek eek eek eek eek.

Japanese: トミーさん！ そうなんですかっ！
English: Moe! I'm right!! Aren't I!?

Japanese: （やっぱり‥‥そう来たか‥‥）
English: (Hmm... So now we're getting closer to the truth.)

Japanese: は‥‥半年前‥‥。 ちょっとした事故が、ね。
English: It was about six months ago... It was just a little accident...

Japanese: それ以上は カンベンしてチョンマゲ！
English: Give me a break... Us old men have accidents. I wear big pants for a reason!
(Six months ago, eh...)

Japanese: トミーさん、教えてください！ “半年前”のコト‥‥
English: C'mon Moe. Tell us what happened six months ago.

Japanese: ちょちょっと今、いそがしいから。 ホラ、ラーメン食べてるし。
English: I'm kinda busy right now. Look... I'm eating a burger as we speak!

Japanese: ううん。 そりゃあ、しかたないですねー。
English: Mmm... That looks so tasty!

Japanese: （話にラーメンがカラむと、 とたんに弱くなるな‥‥）
English: (I just wish we could do something about Moe chewing with his mouth open...)

Japanese: ニイちゃんの言うとおりだ。 あの事故で‥‥
English: It's just like you said. You know... The accident.

Japanese: ‥‥どなたか、 亡くなったんですか？
English: Did someone... die?

Japanese: いや。‥‥でも、ある意味、 そっちのほうがよかったかもな。
English: No, but it would have probably been better if he had.

Japanese: ど‥‥どういうことですか！
English: What!? How would that have been better!?

Japanese: 生きてるよ‥‥今も。
English: He's still alive...

Japanese: ただ、かまれた際に エンズイをキズつけちまってサ。
English: But when he got bit, he suffered massive brain damage.

Japanese: もどらないんだよ ‥‥意識が。
English: He'll never recover from the coma that he's in...

Japanese: イシキが‥‥
English: Coma...?

Japanese: 病院のベッドで 眠りつづけているんだ。
English: All he does now is lay in his bed at the hospital.

Japanese: ‥‥おそらく、 これからもずっと‥‥。
English: And that's all he's ever going to be able to do...

Japanese: そんな‥‥
English: I see...

Japanese: アクロさんと、どういう 関係があるんですか？
English: How is he related to Acro?

Japanese: 弟なんだ。
English: He's his brother.

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: かまれたのは、アクロの弟だ。
English: The person who got bit was Acro's brother.

Japanese: お‥‥弟さん‥‥
English: B-Brother?

Japanese: アクロバットをやってた。 ‥‥アニキと組んでな。
English: They were an acrobat team of brothers, Acro and Bat. Cute nicknames, I thought...

Japanese: すげェコンビだったよ。 それが‥‥いっぺんに、パアだ。
English: Anyways, they were an incredible team... Cut down at the same time...

Japanese: あの‥‥ 弟さん、って‥‥？
English: Umm... Who is Acro's younger brother?

Japanese: 木下　一平（きのしたいっぺい）。 ‥‥バット、って呼ばれてるよ。
English: Sean Dingling... But everyone always called him Bat.

Japanese: アイツ、ミリカにホレててさあ。 気を引こうと必死だったんだ。
English: He fell in love with Regina. Trying to win her love was his downfall.

Japanese: （また‥‥ミリカちゃん、か‥‥）
English: (Everyone seems to fall in love with Regina...)

Japanese: ‥‥半年前だ。 練習中のことだった。
English: Six months ago... While we were practicing...

Japanese: 急にバットが言い出したんだ。 “レオンの芸をさせろ”ってな。
English: All of a sudden Bat blurts out, "Let me perform with Léon!"

Japanese: ど、どうしてそんな‥‥？
English: Why'd he do that?

Japanese: さあ‥‥わからん。 とにかく、事故は起こっちまった。
English: I don't know. But that's what caused the accident.

Japanese: ‥‥あの瞬間のことは、 今でも忘れねえよ。
English: I'll never forget that moment...

Japanese: レオンのヤツ‥‥ フシギな表情をしやがったんだ。
English: It was so strange... Léon had the weirdest look on his face.

Japanese: ‥‥ヤツは‥‥笑ったんだよ。
English: He was... Smiling.

Japanese: ヤツって‥‥ レオン‥‥ですか？
English: He? You mean Léon?

Japanese: そうだ。かみつく一瞬‥‥ あのライオンは、笑ったんだ。
English: Yes. Léon. When he bit down, he was smiling.

Japanese: ニヤッ‥‥、てな。
English: Some sick grin...

Japanese: うそ‥‥！　そ、そんな‥‥。
English: No way!! That's impossible!!

Japanese: （ライオンはニヤリと笑うは、 殺人犯は空に飛んでいくは‥‥）
English: (A smirking lion... A flying murderer...)

Japanese: （なんでこの人、トンでもない 場面ばかり出くわすんだ‥‥？）
English: (Why does it seem that it's always Moe who catches all of these incredible events?)

Japanese: なるほどくん‥‥ ライオンって、笑うの？
English: Nick? Can lions smile?

Japanese: さ、さあ‥‥。
English: Umm...

Japanese: ‥‥警察には知らせなかった。 営業できなくなるからな。
English: We never told the police about the incident. The circus would have shut down if we had.

Japanese: 次の日、団長は‥‥ レオンをライフルで撃ったんだ。
English: The next day, the Ringmaster took Léon out and shot him with a rifle.

Japanese: なになに。“殺人者に告ぐ” ‥‥オダヤカじゃないねえ。
English: Let's see here. "To The Murderer!"... That's far from polite.

Japanese: どこで見つけたんだよ こんなもの。
English: Where did you find the rest of this note anyway?

Japanese: （‥‥トミーさんには 知らせないほうがいいな‥‥）
English: (Maybe it'd be best if I didn't tell Moe where we found it...)

Japanese: やっぱり、いいです。
English: Don't worry about it, Moe.

Japanese: いいんなら、最初ッから 見せるなっての！
English: If I didn't need to worry about it, you shouldn't have shown it to me!!

Japanese: サーカスの動物の名前って、 みんなオレっちがつけてるのよ！
English: You wouldn't know it, but I was responsible for naming all the animals at the circus.

Japanese: へえ！　サルのルーサーとか、 トラのラトーとか？
English: You named Money the Monkey? And Regent, Regina's tiger?

Japanese: うんうん。
English: Guilty as charged.

Japanese: でもさ。“レオン”だけは、 団長がつけたんだ。
English: But Léon got his name from the Ringmaster.

Japanese: そうなんだ。
English: Really?

Japanese: “サルのルーサー”みたいにさ。 人生のキホンは“逆転”なワケよ。
English: He always said "Keep names simple and easy to associate".

Japanese: “逆転”！ いいヒビキですねー。
English: Nick's name is easy to associate, right Wright?

Japanese: だからさ。オレっち、 言ってやったんだ。
English: I always told Russell, "If that's what you think, then call him Lyin' the Lion!"

Japanese: ライオンはゼッタイ “オイラン”だって。
English: It's a great name, don't you think? Imagine if he could talk... "I'm Lyin'!" Aha! Aha!

Japanese: おいらん‥‥
English: Lyin'...

Japanese: そしたら、“このライオンは オスだからダメ”って。
English: But that's when Russell said, "Don't lie to yourself. That name is awful."

Japanese: ‥‥ムカシっから オトナびたヤツだったね。
English: He's always been a very private guy.

Japanese: ここに来たとき、アイツ まだ１０才にもなってなくて。
English: When he came here, he was only about 10 years old.

Japanese: オレたちも、このサーカスを たちあげたばっかりのころでさ。
English: We had just started the Berry Big Circus around the time that Russell took him in.

Japanese: ずいぶん苦しい思いを させちまったよ。
English: These are really hard memories to bring up...

Japanese: アクロさん、すごく カンシャしてましたよ。
English: Acro was so happy to have joined the circus...

Japanese: 団長さんにも‥‥ もちろん、トミーさんにも。
English: He was especially grateful to the Ringmaster... And I'm sure he is grateful to you too.

Japanese: え！　お、オレっち？ ‥‥いやいや。またそんな。
English: Huh? He's grateful to me too? Aww... Shucks. You guys are exaggerating.

Japanese: （クネクネしながら しきりにテレてる‥‥）
English: (He's fidgeting around so much, you can tell that he's embarrassed.)

Japanese: 明るくて、いいヤツだったよ。
English: When you think about it, he really was a good guy.

Japanese: ミリカのコト、好きでね。 ナニかとちょっかいを出してたな。
English: He truly liked Regina. He would try anything to get her attention.

Japanese: そうだったんですか‥‥。
English: He seems like a good guy.

Japanese: あのころは、アクロも よく笑ってたもんだが‥‥。
English: Back then, Acro was always laughing...

Japanese: ‥‥サーカスも、舞台ウラは 楽しいことばかりじゃないね。
English: But I guess that everything backstage at the circus isn't always rosy...

Japanese: そうだね‥‥。
English: I see.

Japanese: それなりにクロウするもんさ。 ヒトを楽しませる、ってのも。
English: It's harder than you think, making people laugh all the time.

Japanese: ～“あのコト”とは～
English: -- "Get Over It"? --

Japanese: ‥‥トミーさん、 教えてもらいますよ。
English: Moe, please tell us what happened six months ago.

Japanese: 半年前‥‥、このサーカスで いったい、何があったのか？
English: What in the world went on at this circus?

Japanese: ま。まあまあ。 そんなコワいカオしないで。
English: OK OK... There's no need to look so scary when you ask me.

Japanese: ホラ。ホラ。 食う？　みそラーメン。
English: Hey! Look over there! Some juicy burgers! Let's eat instead!!

Japanese: あいにくぼくは とんこつ派なんです。
English: Unfortunately, I'm more of a grilled chicken sandwich man myself.

Japanese: うう‥‥。
English: Uhhh...

Japanese: じつは‥‥少し、 ココロあたりがあるんですよ。
English: Actually... I've kinda got an idea of what happened back then.

Japanese: トミーさん、“事故”‥‥と おっしゃいましたね。
English: Moe, you said something about an accident?

Japanese: その事故の原因‥‥、 もしかして、これでは‥‥？
English: This wouldn't happen to be the cause of that accident, would it?

Japanese: どうですか！
English: What about this!?

Japanese: おいしいね！
English: Looks tasty!!

Japanese: な‥‥ナニがですか？
English: W-What!?

Japanese: 決まってるでしょー。 みそラーメン。
English: You know!! The burgers!!

Japanese: （どうやら、いきなり シッパイしたみたいだな‥‥）
English: (It looks like I shot a blank on that attempt...)

Japanese: ‥‥もういっちょ、 行っとくかい？
English: Whaddya say? Another burger sound alright to you?

Japanese: ‥‥ミリカちゃんから 聞きました。
English: I heard a little bit about it from Regina?

Japanese: レオンが芸にシッパイした、って。
English: Léon made a mistake during a performance, right?

Japanese: なんとおおおッ！
English: Whattttttt!?

Japanese: オレっちは、前から言ってたんだ。
English: I told them so many times...

Japanese: あんなキケンな芸は やめるべきだ、って。
English: "You shouldn't be doing dangerous performances like that!"

Japanese: ‥‥レオンの口に アタマを入れる芸ですね？
English: Putting their head inside of Léon's mouth was part of an act!?

Japanese: ああ。ミリカは、レオンを 信じきってたんだ。
English: But Regina believed in Léon.

Japanese: 団長も、それに負けてさ。 ‥‥あの人、親バカだったから。
English: She believed so strongly that the Ringmaster went along... He could never say no to her.

Japanese: いったい、かみつかれたのは だれだったんですか？
English: Out of curiosity, who was bitten?

Japanese: ライオンにアタマをかまれて、 ブジってことはあり得ません。
English: Come on Moe! Don't clam up on me now. Who did Léon bite on the head?

Japanese: ‥‥それは、ちょっと‥‥。 口止めされててさ‥‥。
English: Well... Umm... I promised I wouldn't say anything.

Japanese: くちどめ‥‥？
English: You promised?
