Japanese: そいつも当事者みたいな モンだからなあ‥‥
English: He's involved in this too.

Japanese: （“当事者”か。 それって、もしかして‥‥）
English: (He's "involved" huh? Moe must be talking about...)

Japanese: トミーさん。あなたに口止めを たのんだのは、この人では‥‥？
English: Moe. Is this the person that you promised you wouldn't say anything?

Japanese: ‥‥うおほッほーい！
English: Oooowoooahhhh!!

Japanese: どうしました？
English: What's the matter?

Japanese: みそのカタマリが入ってた。
English: There's a lot of spice on this Cajun burger!

Japanese: ‥‥あの。“口止め”のほうは？
English: Umm... What about the person who you promised to keep silent?

Japanese: わかんないニイちゃんだなあ。 ギリがたいオトコよ？　オレっち。
English: You don't get it yet, do you? What can I say? I have a strong sense of duty.

Japanese: オモシロ半分でペラペラ しゃべるワケにはいかんのよ。
English: Just because I'm funny and talkative doesn't mean that I go around blabbing everything!

Japanese: それに‥‥オレっちに 口止めをたのんだオトコ。
English: I can't tell you who I promised. A promise is a promise you know?

Japanese: ‥‥もちろん、 アクロさんですね。
English: It must have been Acro, right?

Japanese: どどど‥‥ どうしてそれを‥‥！
English: H-H-H-How... How'd you know?

Japanese: いいですか、トミーさん。
English: Don't worry about that, Moe.

Japanese: この“事故”は、団長の事件を 解決するカギかもしれません。
English: Getting to the bottom of this accident may help solve what happened to the Ringmaster.
No... No way.

Japanese: 団長の死の真相を知るためです。 ‥‥聞かせてください。
English: I need to know the truth about what happened to Russell. Please tell me what you know.

Japanese: すまねえな、アクロ‥‥
English: I'm sorry, Acro...

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 テント内　団長室
English: December 29 Big Top Ringmaster's Room

Japanese: 団長さんとマックス、 ここでお話ししてたんだよね。
English: Max and the Ringmaster had their talk in this room.

Japanese: そして団長は、マックスの衣装を 着て、現場に行った‥‥
English: That could have been when the Ringmaster put on Max's costume and went outside.

Japanese: なんでかな‥‥。 サムかった、とか？
English: Why'd he do that? Maybe it was cold or something?

Japanese: あ。これって“エンビ服” っていうんだよ。
English: You may not know this, but they call this a "tailcoat".

Japanese: 知ってるよ。 ‥‥？
English: And they call this "the face of someone who already knew that..." Hmm...

Japanese: どうしたの？
English: What?

Japanese: ポケットから、白い 紙切れがのぞいてるね。
English: A scrap of white paper is sticking out of the coat pocket.

Japanese: ほほお。どれどれ。
English: Huh? Where? Where?

Japanese: ダメだよ、真宵ちゃん。
English: Calm down, Maya.

Japanese: 勝手にヒトのポケット 探っちゃあ。
English: You can't just go rummaging through people's coats!

Japanese: ううう。あたしばっかり ワルモノにする‥‥
English: Ahhh... You always make me feel like I'm doing something wrong...

Japanese: そういえばさ‥‥、
English: Hey, do you see that?

Japanese: このエンビ服‥‥ポケットに、 紙切れが入ってるよね。
English: There's a scrap of paper shoved into the pocket of the tailcoat.

Japanese: そ、それって、 もしかして‥‥
English: You know... I've got a feeling I know what that is...

Japanese: トミーさんにもらった張り紙の 切れっぱしかもしれないよね‥‥
English: I bet that's the other half of the note that Moe gave us.

Japanese: さ、さ、なるほどくん。 サッソク見てみよう！
English: Then let's hurry up and check this thing out, Nick!

Japanese: ‥‥やっぱり！ 破れ目がピッタリ合うよ！
English: I knew it! It fits perfectly with the other piece.

Japanese: なになに。つなげて読むと‥‥
English: What does it say? What does it say?

Japanese: “殺人者に告ぐ。決定的な証拠を あずかっている”‥‥
English: "To The Murderer! I have conclusive evidence of what took place."

Japanese: “今夜１０時、宿舎前の 広場に来られたし”‥‥。
English: "Meet at 10:00 PM tonight at the lodging house plaza."

Japanese: こ‥‥“今夜１０時”って‥‥
English: Tonight at 10:00 PM?

Japanese: 殺人事件が起こった時間だ！
English: That's when the murder took place!

Japanese: 団長さん‥‥だれかに 呼び出されたんだ！
English: Now we need to find out who called out the Ringmaster!!

Japanese: 証拠品《張り紙》のデータを 法廷記録に書きなおした。
English: Note updated in the Court Record.

Japanese: 団長さんのエンビ服だ。
English: The Ringmaster's tailcoat.

Japanese: キレイにアイロンがかかってるね。 シワひとつ、ないよ。
English: It's been expertly cleaned and pressed. It's got one perfect crease.

Japanese: きっと、キレイ好きなヒト だったんだろうね。
English: The Ringmaster must have liked things neat and tidy.

Japanese: ‥‥フツーのケツロンだなあ。 それじゃトミーさんに勝てないよ。
English: This is kinda pointless isn't it? There's no way this beats Moe's room. Now that's cool!

Japanese: （アタマの中で“あひゃ、あひゃ” 笑い声がコダマする‥‥）
English: (All I can hear in my head right now is "Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha!")

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 宿舎前　広場
English: December 29 Lodging House Plaza

Japanese: おお。アンタたちッスか‥‥
English: Oh, it's you two.

Japanese: ‥‥なんか、ものすごく つかれてませんか？
English: You look like you just got hit by a truck. Shouldn't you get some rest?

Japanese: はあ‥‥。 今、ちょっと休憩中ッス。
English: Nah... I'm taking a rest right now, pal.

Japanese: ヘンなピエロのオッサンの 身の上話を聞いていたッス。
English: I've been listening to some crazy clown's life story.

Japanese: 狩魔検事の、たっての おねがい、というコトで。
English: Ms. von Karma told me to come down here and do this for her.

Japanese: そ、それはそれは。
English: Being bossed around by a woman... I know how you feel.

Japanese: あのオッサンのハナシ、 異常につかれるッス。
English: Let me tell you something, pal. Listening to that old clown sucks all your energy.

Japanese: コトあるごとに、ナニかをキタイ するような目で見つめてくるッス。
English: Every time he's done talkin' he looks at you like you should be doing something...

Japanese: ‥‥たぶんそれ、 笑うところですよ。
English: Umm... I think he's waiting for you to laugh at his jokes.

Japanese: ‥‥わかってるッス。
English: I know that, pal.

Japanese: アイソ笑いのしすぎで、 ほっぺのキン肉がつりそうッス。
English: Do you have any idea how much your face hurts if you fake laughing that much?

Japanese: ‥‥でも、 ヒドいなあ、かるま検事。
English: Franziska really set you up bad this time, didn't she?

Japanese: トミーさんのハナシなら、 自分で聞けばいいのに‥‥
English: If you ask me, she should be listening to Moe herself...

Japanese: シッ！
English: No way, pal.

Japanese: かか、カゲグチを言っちゃ ダメッスッ！
English: You're not gonna get me to backbite a woman with a whip. No way.

Japanese: ‥‥どうしてですか？
English: Why are you defending her?

Japanese: かか、狩魔検事は、 いつでもワレワレを見てるッス。
English: Prosecutor von Karma's always got her eyes on us.

Japanese: イチバン見られたくないところで、 サッソウとあらわれるッス！
English: And every time you definitely don't want her to show up... *poof* There she is!!

Japanese: おお‥‥コワいコワい‥‥
English: Don't show up... Don't show up... Don't show up... Don't show up... Don't show up...

Japanese: （かなりイタめつけられてる みたいだな‥‥）
English: (Looks like she's wound him pretty tight...)

Japanese: それに、狩魔検事は今、 ワレワレの真上にいるッス。
English: She's directly above us as we speak.

Japanese: え。 ‥‥どういうことですか？
English: Huh? How's that possible?

Japanese: あのピエロの証言によると、 犯人はここから空中へ消えたッス。
English: According to the clown, the criminal jumped from here and disappeared into the sky.

Japanese: すると、この宿舎の窓の前を 通ることになるッス。
English: If that's what happened, it means the killer passed right by this window, pal.

Japanese: ‥‥なるほど。
English: Oh, I see...

Japanese: だれが住んでるんですか？ 宿舎の上の階には。
English: Who lives in that room behind the window up there?

Japanese: なんでも、３階にアクロバットの 芸人の部屋があるらしいッス。
English: The acrobat's got his room up on the third floor, it seems.

Japanese: これから、狩魔検事が 取り調べをはじめるッス。
English: Pretty soon, Ms. von Karma's gonna start her investigation up there.

Japanese: だから、アクロバットの部屋は 来ちゃダメッスよ！
English: So don't get any ideas of going up to the acrobat's room, got it pal?

Japanese: うううう‥‥かるま検事めッ！
English: *shivers* V-Von Karma...

Japanese: （‥‥取り調べが終わったころ、 行ってみるかな‥‥）
English: (Once she's done with her investigation, I think I'll go up there and check it out.)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 宿舎前　広場
English: December 29 Lodging House Plaza

Japanese: あ、イトノコさん！ あいかわらず、おつかれですね！
English: Hey Detective Gumshoe! I'm sure you did a good job as usual.

Japanese: ‥‥やっとアクロバットの 取り調べが終わったッス。
English: Well, I am done with the investigation of the acrobat. Finally.

Japanese: まったく、狩魔検事ときたら‥‥
English: But with Ms. von Karma...

Japanese: ‥ピピッ‥‥ピピッ‥‥ピピッ‥
English: ...*beep* *beep*... ...*beep* *beep*... ...*beep* *beep*...

Japanese: な、なあに？　なるほどくん。 ‥‥この音‥‥
English: Nick, what is that? That beeping sound?

Japanese: さあ‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: 狩魔検事ッス‥‥
English: It's Ms. von Karma.
Huh?

Japanese: この音が聞こえると、なぜか 狩魔検事がやって来るッス！
English: Every time I hear that sound, she's usually not very far behind!

Japanese: そ、そんなバカな‥‥
English: Some sort of pager or something?

Japanese: じじじ、自分はこれで シツレイするッス！
English: If you don't mind pal, I'm gonna go ahead and get outta here... Quick.

Japanese: じゃッ！
English: See ya, pal!!

Japanese: ‥‥いなくなっちゃったよ。
English: I didn't know that Gumshoe could run that fast! So much for being a flatfoot.

Japanese: （狩魔検事って‥‥ いったい、ナニモノなんだ？）
English: (Never seen a grown man so afraid of a girl still in her teens!)

Japanese: ‥‥それにしても、やっぱり ソトはサムいねえ！
English: Well, let's go inside... It's freezing out here!

Japanese: この、まるで身を切るような‥‥
English: Uh oh... The whi...

Japanese: きゃわわわあっ！
English: OWWWW!!

Japanese: みみ、身を切るような ムチがあ‥‥ッ！
English: That whip could cut right through me...!!

Japanese: か‥‥狩魔検事‥‥ （ホントに、出た‥‥！）
English: Von... Von... Von... Karma!! (She really did appear!!)

Japanese: どうやら、勝負あったわね。 ‥‥成歩堂　龍一！
English: It was a battle today in court, wasn't it... Mr. Phoenix Wright!?

Japanese: い、いきなり、なんだ？
English: Did you have to jump out and scare us like that? What can I do for you?

Japanese: 明日こそ！ ‥‥私のねがいがかなう！
English: Tomorrow will be the day. The day my dream finally comes true!

Japanese: ニッポン中に、ぼくたちの 敗訴のニュースが流れる、って？
English: You mean the story of my defeat at your hands making the national news?

Japanese: “ニッポン”？　‥‥あいかわらず スケールの小さいオトコね‥‥。
English: Ha ha ha... National news? You possess such a small sense of scale...

Japanese: 世界よ世界！　‥‥全世界が キサマのミジメなザマを知るのよ！
English: The global news, Mr. Phoenix Wright! Your miserable plight will be known internationally!

Japanese: （‥‥スケール感がオカシイのは、 そっちだろうが‥‥！）
English: (I think she might be overestimating the importance of a win by just a smidge...)

Japanese: な‥‥なるほどくん！ 御剣検事は‥‥
English: N-N-Nick! Umm... About Edgeworth?

Japanese: ‥‥真宵ちゃん。 前に一度、言っただろ？
English: Maya, I already told you this once. Don't make me do it again.

Japanese: アイツの名前を口にするな、って。
English: Don't bring up his name in front of me again, OK?

Japanese: な‥‥、なるほどくん？
English: N-Nick...?

Japanese: ‥‥狩魔検事。
English: Von Karma?

Japanese: な、何よ‥‥
English: What?

Japanese: 天才検事だかなんだか 知らないけど、
English: I don't know if you are god's gift to prosecutors or not,

Japanese: ぼくは認めないぞ。 きみも‥‥あの男も！
English: but I've had about enough of you!! Him too!

Japanese: ‥‥ど‥‥ どうしちゃったの‥‥？
English: Wha... What happened?

Japanese: フッ‥‥。
English: Hmph.

Japanese: 負けイヌが何をほざいても、 むなしいだけね‥‥。
English: This dog is all bark and no bite. He's already been defeated...

Japanese: とにかく、伝えるべきコトは もうない。
English: Regardless, I have nothing to inform you two of today.

Japanese: 明日がこの国での、 最後の勝負になるわ！
English: Tomorrow will be the greatest courtroom battle this country has ever seen!!

Japanese: なるほどくん‥‥？
English: Nick?

Japanese: ‥‥行こう。 ３階の芸人さんの話を聞くよ。
English: Let's go. We need to talk with the performer on the third floor.

Japanese: 何を怒ってるんだろ‥‥？
English: I'm sorry I brought it up, Nick...

Japanese: 狩魔検事‥‥どうやら、 何かつかんだみたいだな？
English: Von Karma... It appears you got your hands onto something, eh?

Japanese: フッ‥‥キサマにも、 それぐらいはわかるようね。
English: Hah! I'm amazed you picked up on that much.

Japanese: （その勝ちホコったカオを見れば、 だれだってわかるぞ‥‥）
English: (Anyone could. You couldn't hide that look of victory with ten paper bags on your head!)

Japanese: “決定的な証拠”“決定的な証人” ‥‥他に何が必要かしら？
English: I've got "conclusive evidence" and a "conclusive witness"... Need any more hints?

Japanese: “決定的な証人”‥‥ もしかして、アクロバットの‥‥？
English: "A conclusive witness"... You must mean the acrobat, right?

Japanese: ‥‥今、証人として喚問する 手つづきをしているところよ。
English: I'm putting in the summons for him to be called as a witness as we speak.

Japanese: キサマのカンオケに打ちこむ、 最後のクサビね。
English: It's the final nail in your coffin, Mr. Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: （まいったな‥‥）
English: (Yeah, yeah, yeah, I get it already, you want to beat and destroy me.)

Japanese: （なんとしても、 情報をつかまないとな‥‥）
English: (I can't worry about her. I've got to try and find out more information myself.)

Japanese: どうしてそんなに、なるほどくんを 目のカタキにするの！
English: Why do you keep giving Nick the evil eye!?

Japanese: あたしたちの依頼人を ユーザイにしたって‥‥
English: It doesn't matter if you prove the defendant guilty tomorrow...

Japanese: あなたのおとうさんは もう‥‥帰ってこないんだよ！
English: Nothing will be able to bring your dad back!

Japanese: 私の‥‥パパ。 狩魔　豪のコトかしら。
English: My... Dad? You must mean the esteemed Manfred von Karma.

Japanese: そうだよ！ だって‥‥だっておとうさんは！
English: Of course! Your dad! I know you miss him...

Japanese: やれやれ‥‥。もう一発、 ムチを食らいたいようね。
English: Enough out of you... One more word and you'll get a mouthful of whip.

Japanese: 私がいつ、パパのことを 話したかしら‥‥？
English: Now. When did I ever bring up my papa's name in this, or any other conversation...?

Japanese: ‥‥なんだって？ じゃ、じゃあ‥‥いったい？
English: Then... Then what's this "revenge" thing you're talking about?

Japanese: あなたにはわかってるはずよ、 ‥‥成歩堂　龍一。
English: You wouldn't understand, Mr. Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: 私はもう一度、 あの男に会わなければならないの。
English: I have to see "him" again, one more time.

Japanese: あ、“あの男”‥‥？
English: "Him"?

Japanese: そう。 モチロン‥‥御剣　怜侍に！
English: I'm sure you know to whom I refer... Miles Edgeworth!!
What!?

Japanese: ええええええええええええええええ ええええええええええええええッ！
English: Edgeworth!? Mr... EDGEWORTH!?

Japanese: み‥‥御剣‥‥怜侍、だと！
English: M-M-Mi-Miles E-Ed-Edg-Edgeworth!!

Japanese: ‥‥ミツルギ　レイジ‥‥ なぜ、きみがその名前を！
English: Miles Edgeworth... Why would you even bring him up!?

Japanese: ‥‥忘れたのかしら？
English: You haven't forgotten, have you?

Japanese: 天才検事・御剣　怜侍を 育てたのは、いったいだれ？
English: Do you know who it was that trained the gifted prosecutor, Miles Edgeworth?

Japanese: かるま‥‥ごう‥‥
English: Manfred... von... Karma...

Japanese: そのとおり。私のパパよ。
English: Exactly right. It was my papa.

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥あなたと ミツルギ検事は‥‥？
English: That means that Edgeworth was...

Japanese: そうね‥‥。
English: Right again.

Japanese: レイジは、私の 弟‥‥のようなものかしら。
English: Miles was like a little brother to me.

Japanese: うううう‥‥ミツルギ検事、 なるほどくんと同い年なのに‥‥。
English: Huh? Little brother? Edgeworth and Nick are the same age...
(Edgeworth...)

Japanese: （ぼくが、この世界に足を 踏み入れるキッカケを作った男）
English: (The man who inspired me to become an attorney.)

Japanese: （‥‥いくつもの事件を アイツと戦った‥‥）
English: (I fought against him in a few cases...)

Japanese: （そして‥‥あの事件が 解決したあと‥‥）
English: (But after "that" case was over...)

Japanese: （アイツは‥‥“死んだ”）
English: (He "vanished"...)

Japanese: ‥‥あなたが殺したのよ。
English: It's your fault he is gone.

Japanese: え！
English: HUH!?

Japanese: そうでしょう？ ‥‥成歩堂　龍一。
English: It's the truth, isn't it Mr. Phoenix Wright?

Japanese: なるほどくん！　どういうことッ！
English: Nick! What does she mean!?

Japanese: あの事件から‥‥ アイツのようすはおかしくなった。
English: After that case, Edgeworth was in a peculiar state, and he got worse everyday.

Japanese: 法廷に立つことが ほとんどなくなって‥‥
English: He never set foot into court again...

Japanese: ‥‥ある日‥‥。 アイツは消えたんだ。
English: And then one day, he just vanished.

Japanese: 検事局に、カンタンな メモを残して。
English: All he left was a simple note at the prosecutor's office.

Japanese: “検事・御剣　怜侍は死を選ぶ”
English: "Prosecutor Miles Edgeworth chooses death."

Japanese: あれからもう、１年たつ。
English: That was one year ago.

Japanese: きみがふるさとに帰って すぐのことだったんだ。
English: It was right after you left to go back home.

Japanese: ‥‥みつるぎけんじ‥‥ 死んじゃったの？
English: Mr. Edgeworth... He's dead?

Japanese: 私は信じない。
English: I don't believe it.

Japanese: 彼は生きている。 この世界のどこかで‥‥かならず。
English: He's still alive. I'm sure of it... Somewhere in this world, he's still alive.

Japanese: “検事・御剣　怜侍は死んだ”？ ‥‥そりゃあそうよ！
English: "Prosecutor Miles Edgeworth chooses death?" Of course he did!

Japanese: だって、成歩堂　龍一！ アナタごときに敗れたのだから！
English: You ruined his reputation as a Prosecutor! You effectively killed the Prosecutor in him.

Japanese: ‥‥狩魔のカオに、ぬぐいきれない ドロをぬってくれたのだから！
English: Just like your victory muddied the honorable name Von Karma!

Japanese: 私はもう一度彼に会って‥‥
English: I'm going to find him...

Japanese: ケジメをつけてやるわ！ ‥‥私の手で、ね‥‥。
English: Then I'm going to teach him the difference between right and wrong with my own hands!

Japanese: あの。これなんですけど‥‥
English: What about this?

Japanese: きゃおうッ！
English: OWWW!!

Japanese: ‥‥またぶたれた。
English: She hit me again.

Japanese: ヘタにちょっかい 出さないほうがいいよ。
English: Maybe you should hold off on the wild goose chases.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 宿舎１階　トミーの部屋
English: December 29 Lodging Hall 1st Floor Moe's Room

Japanese: ‥‥トミーさん、いないな。
English: Moe's not here...

Japanese: いるのなら、ドアの そとからでもわかるもんね。
English: If he was here, you would have been able to tell even before you stepped into his room.

Japanese: あひゃあひゃ笑い声が 聞こえるから。
English: I'm sure you would've heard him laughing away... "Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha!"

Japanese: ‥‥ヒトリで、いったい ナニがおかしいんだろ？
English: What do you think he is laughing at when he's all by himself?

Japanese: きっと、新しいギャグを 考えてるんじゃないかな。
English: I always thought he was just thinking up new jokes.

Japanese: （シゴト熱心‥‥だなあ）
English: (Hmm... He must really love his work...)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 宿舎１階　トミーの部屋
English: December 29 Lodging Hall 1st Floor Moe's Room

Japanese: トミーさん、いないね。
English: Hmm... Moe's not here.

Japanese: ‥‥でも、 なんか物音がするぞ。
English: What's that!? I hear something...

Japanese: えっ。 や、やめてよなるほどくん‥‥
English: S-Stop it, Nick... You're scaring me!

Japanese: キキーッ！
English: Oooo-Ooooh!

Japanese: るる、 ルーサーだよなるほどくん！
English: Nick!! It's Money!!

Japanese: ‥‥サルめ、 なんか、持ってるぞ‥‥。
English: That monkey's gotten his hands on something again...

Japanese: あ、アレだよ！　ミリカちゃんの “ダイジなモノ”‥‥！
English: That's it! That's the thing "that means a lot" to Regina, remember?

Japanese: （‥‥よし！　弁護士として サルと勝負だ‥‥！）
English: (Alright! Time to take on this monkey, attorney-style!)

Japanese: ‥‥返せコラ！
English: Give it back, monkey-brain!

Japanese: キッキッキー！
English: Oooo-Ooooo-Ooooh!

Japanese: ミリカちゃんが ダイジにしてるんだ！
English: That means a lot to Regina!!

Japanese: オマエも男なら、 女の子を泣かせちゃ‥‥
English: A real man wouldn't make a little girl cry!!

Japanese: キキキキーッ！
English: Oo-Oo-Oo-Oo-Oooh!

Japanese: きゃあっ！
English: Yikes!!

Japanese: うわっ！
English: WAAHHH!!

Japanese: ‥‥“男”対“男”として、 アツく語りかけてみたのに‥‥。
English: I tried to have a man-to-man talk with him... I really did.

Japanese: ルーサー、“男”っていうより “オス”だからねえ‥‥
English: You know... Man-to-man isn't really accurate. It was more like man-to-monkey.

Japanese: ステイ！　ルーサー！　ステイ！
English: Stay! Money! Stay!

Japanese: ‥‥キキーッ？
English: Oo-Ooooh?

Japanese: おお。通じてるぞ！
English: Hey, it's working!!

Japanese: そうかなあ。 最後が“？”だったよ‥‥
English: Umm... It sounded like he was confused at the end of that "Ooooh".

Japanese: ステイ‥‥わわっ！
English: Stay... AHHHH!!

Japanese: キキキキーッ！
English: Oooooo-Oo-Oooh!

Japanese: きゃあっ！
English: Yikes!!

Japanese: うわっ！
English: WAAHHH!!

Japanese: ‥‥ミリカちゃんのマネ してみたのに‥‥。
English: I knew that acting like Regina would do the trick!

Japanese: ウキャーッ！
English: Oooh-Oooh-Oh-Oh!

Japanese: キキキキーッ？
English: Oo-Ooooh?

Japanese: ウキキッキー！
English: Wa-Oooh-Oooh!!

Japanese: キキキキーッ！
English: Oooooo-Oo-Oooh!

Japanese: きゃあっ！
English: Yikes!!

Japanese: うわっ！
English: WAAHHH!!

Japanese: ‥‥“サル”対“サル”として、 アツく語りかけてみたのに‥‥。
English: I tried to have a monkey-to-monkey talk with him... I really did.

Japanese: あっ、なるほどくん！ ‥‥それは？
English: Nick! What's that?

Japanese: ああ。すれちがいざまに 取り返してやったよ。
English: I swiped it while Money was distracted...

Japanese: へええ！　なかなかヤルね、 なるほどくん！
English: Really? You're really on the ball today, Nick!

Japanese: ね。ね。ちょっと見せてよ！
English: Lemme see it... Lemme see it!!

Japanese: ‥‥ん？　どうするんだ？
English: Huh? You can see it fine from where you are.

Japanese: ちょっとね、着てみたいの。 ミリカちゃんの衣装‥‥。
English: You know what I mean... I really want to try on Regina's costume...

Japanese: （‥‥やれやれ‥‥）
English: (Maybe then they'll take you in at the circus and I can get some peace and quiet...)

Japanese: あ‥‥あれ？
English: Hmm...

Japanese: どうした？
English: What's the matter now?

Japanese: ぜ‥‥ゼンゼン、 サイズが合わないよ‥‥。
English: It doesn't fit me at all!

Japanese: たしかに‥‥と言うより、
English: Oh well, guess it's time for you to lay off the burgers!

Japanese: そもそも、人間の着るモノとは 思えないな‥‥。
English: Not to mention it doesn't look like something any girl I know would actually wear...

Japanese: 《ステージ衣装》を ルーサーから取りもどした。
English: Stage Costume added to the Court Record.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 宿舎３階　アクロの部屋
English: December 29 Lodging Hall 3rd Floor Acro's Room

Japanese: ‥‥あなたが、成歩堂さんですね。
English: You must be Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: は、はい。
English: Y-Yes.

Japanese: はじめまして。オレ、木下　大作 （きのしただいさく）といいます。
English: Pleased to meet you. I'm Ken Dingling.

Japanese: ‥‥ここでは、 “アクロ”と呼ばれていますが。
English: But here at the circus, everyone just calls me Acro.

Japanese: アクロ‥‥さん。
English: Mr... Acro.

Japanese: あの。なんでぼくの名前を‥‥？
English: Umm... How do you know my name?

Japanese: 刑事さんが教えてくれました。 きっと現れるだろう、って。
English: The detective told me. He said you'd definitely show up here.

Japanese: アクロさんも‥‥ このサーカスの？
English: Acro, you're a member of this circus as well?

Japanese: ‥‥そうですね。ツナわたりや、 空中ブランコをやっていました。
English: That's right. I mainly perform on the tightrope or the flying trapeze.

Japanese: 今はこんな ‥‥車イス生活ですけどね。
English: But nowadays... All I perform in is my wheelchair.

Japanese: あれ‥‥成歩堂さん。 また来たんですか？
English: Ahh... Mr. Wright. Back again I see.

Japanese: また来ちゃいました。
English: Well he did say, "I'll be back." Wait... or was that someone else?

Japanese: （アクロさんは‥‥足のケガの 原因をかくしている！）
English: (We're back because Acro's hiding why his legs were injured!)

Japanese: （ケガは半年前の“事故”‥‥）
English: (He was hurt in the accident six months ago!)

Japanese: （今なら‥‥わかるような 気がするな）
English: (It would seem that he knows that we know...)

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら‥‥ 何か、お話があるようですね。
English: Well well... It seems you've got things you want to talk about, so fire away.

Japanese: アクロさんは、 どうしてサーカスに？
English: Acro, why did you join the circus?

Japanese: 小さいころ、両親が事業に 失敗しましてね。
English: When I was a kid, my parents failed miserably at business.

Japanese: 夜逃げしてしまったんですよ。 ‥‥子供をおいて。
English: Then one night, they decided to run away from it all... Without me.

Japanese: たった１人の親せきが、 団長の立見さんでした。
English: The only person who was willing to take my parent's place was the Ringmaster.

Japanese: 団長には、本当に世話に なりました。‥‥本当に。
English: The Ringmaster took such incredible care of me. He was truly a lifesaver.

Japanese: いいヒトだったんですね。 ‥‥団長さん。
English: It seems like the Ringmaster was truly a saint.

Japanese: ええ。 ‥‥オレ、決めてたんです。
English: He was... That's why I decided to do something very important.

Japanese: オレは一生をかけて、 あの人にむくいるんだ、って。
English: I decided that I would devote my entire life to finding a way to repay him...

Japanese: まさか‥‥ こんなことになるなんて！
English: And now... Look how someone repaid him for all the good in his life!!

Japanese: そうだったんですか‥‥。
English: It's such a shame.

Japanese: ‥‥あの人は、 やさしすぎたんです。
English: Sometimes I think that he was almost too kind...

Japanese: オレたち団員にも‥‥ 自分のムスメにも。
English: Perhaps he was too kind to his performers... Perhaps he was too kind to his daughter.

Japanese: （ムスメ‥‥って、 ミリカちゃんだよな‥‥）
English: (Hmm... I wonder if he spoiled Regina?)

Japanese: ミリカちゃん、カワイイですよね！ 純粋で‥‥天使みたい。
English: Regina is so cute!! She's truly a princess.

Japanese: ジュンスイ‥‥ですか。 ‥‥それが、イイことだとでも？
English: Truly a princess? Are you sure that's a good thing?
Umm...

Japanese: （なぜだ？　ミリカちゃんに対して 悪意のようなものを感じる‥‥）
English: (Hmm... Do I detect a hint of a grudge against Regina...)

Japanese: あの‥‥。 どうして車イスに？
English: Umm... I'm sorry to ask, but why are you in a wheel chair?

Japanese: 足の神経をやられたんです。 ‥‥ズタズタに。
English: The nerves in my legs were badly damaged.

Japanese: 歩くことも‥‥？
English: And you can't walk now?

Japanese: 立ち上がることもできません。
English: I can't even stand now.

Japanese: ここは３階ですから、１人で 宿舎を出ることもできません。
English: And since I live on the third floor, I can't even leave this building by myself.

Japanese: そうなんですか‥‥。
English: That's awful...

Japanese: やっぱり、アクロバットが原因で？
English: The accident happened during an acrobatics session, right?
Umm...

Japanese: （サイコ・ロック‥‥！）
English: (A Psyche-Lock!)

Japanese: （ケガの原因‥‥アクロバットじゃ ないみたいだな‥‥）
English: (It doesn't seem like Acro's injuries were acrobatic in nature...)

Japanese: どうかしましたか？　成歩堂さん。
English: What's on your mind, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: い、いえ‥‥。
English: Well...

Japanese: そのケガは、いつごろ？
English: Exactly when were you injured?

Japanese: もう、半年ほど前になりますか。 練習中に、ちょっとね。
English: It's almost been six months since I was hurt. I injured my legs during practice.

Japanese: （半年前‥‥何かあったんだ！ このサーカスで‥‥）
English: (Six months ago... What in the world went on at this circus then!?)

Japanese: ‥‥リハビリテーションも してますからね。
English: I'm still undergoing rehabilitation for my injuries.

Japanese: 今は立つこともできませんが、 いずれ歩けるようにはなりますよ。
English: I still can't stand, but the doctor's say that I'll be able to recover and walk again.

Japanese: いったい‥‥どんな事故 だったんですか？
English: Out of curiosity, what kind of accident was it again?

Japanese: ‥‥お話しするほどのコトは ありませんよ。
English: I don't really have much more to add beyond that.

Japanese: （アクロさんがかくしている “事故”の原因‥‥）
English: (Acro's hiding what really caused his "accident".)

Japanese: （団長の事件と、関係が あるんだろうか‥‥？）
English: (Maybe it's related to what happened to the Ringmaster?)

Japanese: そういえばアクロさん、 きのうは部屋にいませんでしたね？
English: I stopped by yesterday and noticed that you weren't in your room.

Japanese: きのうは１日、 病院へ行っていました。
English: I was at the hospital all day yesterday.

Japanese: ああ‥‥リハビリですか。 じゃあ、事件のことは？
English: Ahh... You went there for rehabilitation? What about the murder?

Japanese: もちろん、知ってましたよ。
English: Of course I knew about it...

Japanese: 警察に、ひととおりの話をして、 病院へ行かせてもらったんです。
English: I spoke with the police before they allowed me to go to the hospital.

Japanese: ‥‥検事さんから話を聞くまで、 オレ、ユメだと思ってたんですよ。
English: Before I got the call from the prosecutor, I was convinced that it was all a dream.
Huh?

Japanese: 自分でも信じられなかったから。 アレを見たとき‥‥。
English: I just couldn't believe it. When I saw what I saw...

Japanese: “アレ”を見た‥‥。 （ヒジョーにイヤな予感がする）
English: "What you saw"...? (Jeez, that sounded really ominous...)

Japanese: アクロさん‥‥いったい、 何を見たんですか？
English: What did you see, Acro?

Japanese: あの夜‥‥
English: That night...

Japanese: ベッドで寝ていたら、窓の下で 大きな音がしたんです。
English: I was in bed sleeping when I heard a huge sound coming from below my window.

Japanese: そうか。‥‥現場って、 この窓の下だもんね。
English: I see... The scene of the crime was right below your window.

Japanese: それで、窓のほうを見たんです。 そうしたら‥‥
English: That's when I looked out the window...

Japanese: そ、そうしたら？
English: What did you see?

Japanese: 彼が通りすぎていきました ‥‥下から、上へ。
English: He was flying straight up into the air...

Japanese: か‥‥彼？
English: H-He?

Japanese: マックス・ギャラクティカです。
English: Maximillion Galactica.

Japanese: うえええええっ！
English: Whaaatttt!?

Japanese: （‥‥やっぱり‥‥ そうなっちゃうのか‥‥）
English: (That's what I thought he'd say...)

Japanese: ‥‥まちがいなく マックスさんでしたか？
English: You're absolutely positive that it was Max you saw flying?

Japanese: ‥‥ええ。 まちがいありませんね。
English: I am absolutely sure. There's no doubt in my mind.

Japanese: な、なるほどくうん‥‥
English: N-Nick...

Japanese: ‥‥オレのこの足は、 レオンにやられました。
English: My legs were injured by Léon.

Japanese: 半年前。
English: Six months ago.

Japanese: 弟は‥‥バットは、ミリカに 賭けを持ちかけたんですよ。
English: My younger brother, Bat, had a dare with Regina.

Japanese: 賭け‥‥？
English: A dare?

Japanese: バカげた賭けでした。
English: An absurd dare.

Japanese: “自分がミリカのかわりに レオンの芸をやったら‥‥
English: "If I can put my head inside of Léon's mouth like you do..."

Japanese: いっしょに映画へ行こう” って‥‥。
English: "You have to go to the movies with me on a date."

Japanese: そ‥‥そんな、あぶない‥‥！
English: That's insane!! Didn't he know how dangerous that is!?

Japanese: オレたちもバカだった‥‥。
English: We all thought he was being stupid too...

Japanese: でも、あのライオンはもう、 年老いていた。
English: But that lion was very old to begin with,

Japanese: それに、もう何年も その芸をやっていた‥‥。
English: and age brought with it countless experience in doing that very trick.

Japanese: つい、油断してしまったんです。
English: Unfortunately, this particular time, I guess Léon wasn't ready or willing.

Japanese: それで‥‥シッパイした。
English: And that's when the accident happened?

Japanese: たかが映画のために、 弟は‥‥
English: He just wanted to take her out... To the movies...

Japanese: かわいそう‥‥バットさん‥‥
English: Poor Bat...

Japanese: オレもカッとなって‥‥ レオンに飛びかかった。
English: When Léon chomped down, I jumped towards him.

Japanese: かなうワケないのに。 それで‥‥このザマです。
English: Then Léon attacked me and that's how I ended up...

Japanese: ‥‥あの。バットさんは‥‥？
English: What about Bat?

Japanese: 今も、眠りつづけています。
English: He's still in a coma.

Japanese: きのう、オレが行ったのは、 アイツがいる病院だったんですよ。
English: I went to the hospital yesterday to visit him.

Japanese: そうだったんですか‥‥。
English: I see...

Japanese: アイツが目をさますのを待つ‥‥
English: I'm still waiting for him to open his eyes again...

Japanese: ‥‥それが、オレの新しい 生きる意味‥‥ですかね。
English: And that's the reason why I keep going.

Japanese: バットさんとミリカさんは‥‥
English: Bat and Regina...

Japanese: ずいぶん仲が よかったものですよ。
English: They were such great friends.

Japanese: ‥‥そうだ。 コイツ、見てください。
English: Oh yeah... I wanted you to take a look at this.

Japanese: これは‥‥？
English: What is it?

Japanese: 弟がレオンにおそわれたとき、 巻いていたスカーフです。
English: This is the scarf my brother was wearing when Léon attacked him.

Japanese: ひどい‥‥。血が‥‥
English: Gross... It's covered in blood.

Japanese: そのスカーフ‥‥。
English: This scarf...

Japanese: 事故があった日、ミリカが 弟にプレゼントしたものなんです。
English: Was a present from Regina to my brother on the day of the accident.

Japanese: そうなんですか‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: あの瞬間‥‥ヤツは‥‥ 笑っていました。
English: When he did it, he looked like he was smiling...

Japanese: ‥‥ヤツ？
English: He?

Japanese: レオンですよ。もちろん。
English: Léon, obviously.

Japanese: え！
English: What!?

Japanese: バットのアタマに かみつく瞬間‥‥
English: When he bit down on Bat's head...

Japanese: ヤツは、カオをゆがめて‥‥ 笑ったんです。
English: The expression on Léon's face looked like a grin.

Japanese: なるほどくん！
English: Nick!

Japanese: ああ！　‥‥トミーさんも、 そんなことを言っていた‥‥。
English: I know!! Moe said the same thing...

Japanese: いったい、どういう ことなの‥‥
English: What do you think it all means?

Japanese: そのスカーフ‥‥ こちらにわたしていただこう。
English: I'll be taking that scarf if you don't mind...

Japanese: か‥‥狩魔検事‥‥。
English: Von Karma?

Japanese: 話は聞かせてもらったわ。 ‥‥それをわたしなさい。
English: I've already heard everything! So hand over the scarf.

Japanese: で、でもこんなスカーフ、 事件の証拠には‥‥
English: But the scarf is evidence in the trial!

Japanese: それは、私が判断するわ。
English: That is for me to decide.

Japanese: ‥‥では、アクロ氏。 したくをおねがいしましょうか。
English: I think we should begin our preparations now, Acro.
Preparations?

Japanese: アクロ氏には、明日の法廷で 証言台についてもらうわ。
English: I've served a summons on Acro to appear in court tomorrow as a witness.

Japanese: ‥‥検事局で話を うかがっておきたいの。
English: Acro, we'll talk more at the prosecutor's office.

Japanese: アクロさんが‥‥証人！
English: Acro... A witness!?

Japanese: さあ。アクロ氏。 検事局まで、ご足労ねがうわ。
English: Come Acro. Let's go to the office.

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。
English: Yes, ma'am.

Japanese: ど、どうするの‥‥？ だいじょうぶ？　あした‥‥
English: Now what do we do, Nick? How are we going to handle tomorrow?

Japanese: ‥‥シンパイいらないよ。 なんとかするから。
English: Don't worry about it. I'll figure something out...

Japanese: おッ！　その 自信マンマンのカオ！
English: Look at you all full of confidence!!

Japanese: きっと、何か手がかりを つかんだんだね！
English: You must have found something you can use!

Japanese: （弁護士は、ピンチのときほど ふてぶてしく笑うもんだからな）
English: (This is all beginning to come together now...)

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: ‥‥アクロさん、 これを見てください。
English: Acro, please take a look at this photo for us...

Japanese: ‥‥そうですか。 団長は、こんなサイゴを‥‥
English: No problem... Ahh... This is how it ended for him... Such a shame...

Japanese: （表情はおだやかだけど‥‥）
English: (He seems awfully calm...)

Japanese: （目が‥‥深い悲しみで イタイほど、すんでいる‥‥）
English: (But his eyes have a look of sorrow and profound pain...)

Japanese: オレの足が こんなじゃなければ‥‥
English: If my legs weren't like this...

Japanese: こんなこと、ゼッタイ 防げたハズなのに‥‥！
English: I would have been able to prevent this from happening! That's an absolute certainty!

Japanese: アクロさん‥‥
English: Acro...

Japanese: マックス・ギャラクティカ ‥‥ですか。
English: Maximillion Galactica, right?

Japanese: すみませんが‥‥彼のことは 考えないようにしているんです。
English: You'll have to forgive me, but I try not to think about him.

Japanese: （アクロさん‥‥そっと 目をそらしたぞ）
English: (Acro won't even look at it...)

Japanese: なんだか、とても苦しそう‥‥。
English: It looks like something is really weighing on him.

Japanese: ああ‥‥。ルーサーなら、 オレの友だちですよ。
English: Heh... Money is a great friend to me.

Japanese: ええ。あそこにある宝の山 ‥‥彼のコレクションでしょう？
English: That pile of "treasure" over there is his collection, huh?

Japanese: そう。ルーサーのヤツ、本当に なんでも持ってくるんですよ。
English: It is indeed. Money will bring anything back with him.

Japanese: ふふ。カワイイですよね！
English: Aww... That's so cute!

Japanese: こんな身ナリで 女の子にはモテませんけどね。
English: Yeah, I'm not great with the ladies...

Japanese: なぜか、動物たちには 好かれるんですよ。
English: But I seem to be pretty popular with the animals.

Japanese: ‥‥それは？
English: Hmm... What's this?

Japanese: い、いえ。ぼくたちが 聞きたいんですよ。
English: That's what we want to know.

Japanese: 事件の日の朝、食堂に 張ってあったんです。
English: It was posted in the cafeteria the morning before the murder.

Japanese: 食堂に‥‥？
English: In the... Cafeteria?

Japanese: （‥‥な、なんだ‥‥？ 急にキビしいカオして‥‥）
English: (W-What happened? He suddenly looks incredibly serious...)

Japanese: そいつのコトなら、あの子に 聞いてみたらどうですか？
English: If it's got something to do with her, then you should go straight to the source.
Her?

Japanese: ええ。‥‥ミリカに。
English: Regina. Ask her about it.

Japanese: これ、見てください！
English: What do you think of this!?

Japanese: すみませんけどオレ、 こんなカラダですから。
English: I'm sorry, but in my present physical condition...

Japanese: この部屋のそとのコト、 あんまり知らないんですよ。
English: I don't really know much about what goes on outside of this room.

Japanese: あ。ごめんなさい‥‥。
English: Oh... We're sorry...

Japanese: ベツに、あやまらなくても いいですよ。
English: Don't worry about it. No need to apologize.

Japanese: ‥‥マックス、ですか。
English: You want to know about Max?

Japanese: サーカスの他の連中は キライみたいですけどね。
English: Well, his colleagues in the circus all seem to hate him.

Japanese: え‥‥じゃ、アクロさんは？
English: What about you, Acro?

Japanese: モチロン、彼にもモンダイは ありますよ。
English: I've got my issues with him as well.

Japanese: ただ、彼のココロには ダイヤモンドがかがやいている。
English: But he just also happens to have a diamond shining in his soul.

Japanese: だいやもんど？
English: A diamond shining in his soul?

Japanese: 芸人としてのホコリ、 というのでしょうか‥‥？
English: I guess you could say it is his pride as a performer.

Japanese: そいつは、オレたちに なかったものです。
English: We didn't have that pride in ourselves before Max arrived.

Japanese: このサーカスにアイツが 来て、よかったと思いますよ。
English: Honestly, I think he brought something wonderful to this circus when he came.

Japanese: アクロさん‥‥。
English: Acro...

Japanese: （この人‥‥他の団員とは ちがうフンイキがあるよな‥‥）
English: (This guy is really different from the other members of the circus...)

Japanese: 成歩堂さん。‥‥あなたを見て、 だれかに似ていると思いました。
English: Mr. Wright. I just realized who you remind me of...

Japanese: ‥‥わかりましたよ。 この検事さんです。
English: You're just like this prosecutor.

Japanese: なな、なんですって！
English: W-W-W-W-WHAT!?

Japanese: あなたの目‥‥この検事さんと 同じ光をしているんですよ。
English: You and the prosecutor have the same glint in your eyes.

Japanese: じょ、冗談じゃない！ あんなヤツといっしょに‥‥
English: Y-You're joking, right!? You think I'm like that whip carrying demon!?

Japanese: ‥‥そんなに コーフンしないでください。
English: Whoa... No need to get so hostile about it.

Japanese: ‥‥おふたりの見ている方向は ちがうのかもしれません‥‥今は。
English: You two may view things from very different perspectives... for now.

Japanese: でも、あなたたちの目は、 同じ色をしています。
English: But you both see the world through the same color-tinted glasses.

Japanese: ‥‥オレ、人を見る目だけは 自信あるんですよ。
English: I know because when I look at people, their eyes are the only things I can believe in.

Japanese: へええ。 なるほどくんがねえ‥‥。
English: Hmm... Nick and Von Karma, huh...

Japanese: ミリカ‥‥
English: Regina...

Japanese: カッコイイですよねー。
English: She's cool, isn't she?

Japanese: あんなカワイイ女の子が、 猛獣使いなんて！
English: And can you believe such a cute girl is an animal tamer!?

Japanese: 使うのは、猛獣だけでは ないようですけどね。
English: It seems animals are not the only thing she tames.
Huh?

Japanese: マックスや団長、 それに、ベン‥‥。
English: Max, the Ringmaster, Ben...

Japanese: みんな彼女に、いいように 使われていますね。
English: She's got them all under her thumb.

Japanese: ‥‥そ、 そんな言いかたは‥‥
English: W-Whaddya mean by that?

Japanese: ‥‥そうですね。 ちょっと言いすぎました。
English: Hmm... Maybe I went a bit overboard.

Japanese: 無邪気なんですよ、彼女は。 ダイジに育てられましたから。
English: It's just Regina's innocence. She was incredibly sheltered as a child.

Japanese: ホントにそうですよねー。
English: I'd say it seems that way.

Japanese: だから‥‥ザンコクなんです。
English: That's why she can be so cruel.
What?

Japanese: 彼女は、猛獣たちと同じです。
English: Regina is just like the animals she claims to tame.

Japanese: 無邪気で‥‥ だからこそ、ザンコクだ。
English: She's innocent, thus she can be cruel.

Japanese: （アクロさんとミリカちゃん ‥‥何があったんだ‥‥？）
English: (I wonder what happened between Acro and Regina...)

Japanese: ベンさんのことですけど‥‥。
English: I was hoping you could shed some light on Ben...

Japanese: ‥‥アイツは、ここに いるしかない。
English: Well, he'll always be here.

Japanese: そして、ここにいる以上、 腹話術をつづけるしかない。
English: And as long as he's here, he really can only continue to be a ventriloquist.

Japanese: それが彼にとって いいことなのか‥‥
English: Whether that's a good thing for him or not...

Japanese: オレには なんとも言えませんね。
English: I'm afraid I can't really say.

Japanese: 団長は、トミーさんのことを 信じていました。
English: The Ringmaster really believed in Moe. One-hundred percent.

Japanese: これからこのサーカスが どうなっていくのか‥‥？
English: What will happen to the circus now that the Ringmaster's gone...?

Japanese: トミーさんしだい ‥‥でしょうね。
English: The beginning of the Lawrence Curls era I suppose.

Japanese: （だいじょうぶかねえ ‥‥あんなオジさんで‥‥）
English: (I wonder if it's really alright to let Moe run the show...)

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂さんは、トミーさんを 知らないんですよ。
English: Mr. Wright, you don't know much about Moe, I suspect.

Japanese: うう‥‥ココロを読まれた‥‥。
English: You can see right through me.

Japanese: あーあ。弁護士シッカクだね、 なるほどくん！
English: Aww Nick! You can't let him get to you like that! You're an attorney!

Japanese: 団長は‥‥ 親以上の存在です。
English: The Ringmaster... He was even more than a father to me.

Japanese: ここに引き取ってもらった当時、 サーカスの経営はヒドかった。
English: When he took me in, the circus was in bad shape.

Japanese: オレたちをやしなうヨユウがない のは、見ていてよくわかりました。
English: It was obvious that he had no real way of supporting us.
(Who does he mean by us...?)

Japanese: だから、始めたんですか？ ‥‥アクロバット。
English: Is that why you started with acrobatics?

Japanese: そのとき、 オレは９才だったけど‥‥
English: Back then, I was only nine years old.

Japanese: 団長にムリを言って、 やらせてもらったんです。
English: I begged the Ringmaster to let me try, and he finally gave in.

Japanese: そんな子供がサーカスなんて、 本当はマズいんだけど。
English: Having a kid in the circus was probably the last thing in the world that he wanted...

Japanese: 何か役に立ちたかった。 ‥‥団長のために。
English: I just wanted to be helpful. So I could help the Ringmaster.

Japanese: いい話ですねえ。
English: That's such a nice story.

Japanese: ‥‥団長が こんなことになって‥‥
English: I can't believe what happened to the Ringmaster... Such a wonderful man... What a pity.

Japanese: 生きているイミを 見失いそうですよ。
English: I have no clue what to do with my life now.

Japanese: そ、そんな‥‥
English: I'm sorry...

Japanese: ‥‥オレにとっては、 このサーカスがすべてだから。
English: This circus meant everything to me.

Japanese: でも‥‥、 シンパイはいりませんよ。
English: But don't worry about me...

Japanese: オレにだって、まだ やるべきことはあるから。
English: There are still things that I have left to do in this world.

Japanese: オレ、最近はずっと この部屋にいますから。
English: Lately, I've been confined to this room,

Japanese: サーカスのそとのコトなんて、 ゼンゼン知らないんですよ。
English: so I don't have a clue what's going on with the circus.

Japanese: ～車イスのこと～
English: -- About the Wheelchair --

Japanese: ‥‥聞かせてもらいますよ。 なぜ、そんなケガをしたのか‥‥？
English: I have to ask you, how were you injured?

Japanese: お話ししたでしょう？ これは、練習中の事故です。
English: I'm sorry, I thought we talked about this? It was an accident that happened during practice.

Japanese: “練習中の事故”‥‥ですか？
English: An accident during practice?

Japanese: ええ‥‥アクロバットには、 キケンがつきものですから。
English: Yes. Unfortunately acrobats are prone to all sorts of injuries.

Japanese: （ちがう‥‥それならば、 ヒミツにする必要はない！）
English: (He's lying... If that were the real cause, he'd have no reason to keep it secret.)

Japanese: アクロさん。あなたのケガ、 本当はこれが原因なのでは‥‥？
English: Acro, are you really telling me that a practice accident was the cause of your injury?

Japanese: さあ。聞かせてください、 アクロさん！
English: Alright Acro... Just tell me the truth.

Japanese: ‥‥その前に、オレのほうから 聞いていいですか？
English: Do you mind if I ask you a question first?

Japanese: なんですか？
English: What do you want to know?

Japanese: そんなモノで、どうやって 大ケガできるって言うんですか？
English: Do you really think I could get an injury like this from something like that?

Japanese: （い、いきなりミスした らしいな‥‥）
English: (Yikes! It seems I'm off-track with this one...)

Japanese: これがロープの上ならば、 もうあなたはおしまいです。
English: If that's how you handle yourself, I suggest a net if you ever try the tightrope.

Japanese: （ここはロープの上じゃない。 考えるんだ‥‥もう一度）
English: (He's right. I slipped up just now... I've got to rethink things.)

Japanese: レオン‥‥。
English: Léon...

Japanese: ‥‥半年前、 あなたは、ライオンにおそわれた。
English: Six months ago, you were attacked by the lion.

Japanese: ケガは、そのときのものです。
English: That's when you were injured.

Japanese: （どうやら‥‥ぼくの推理は、 まちがってないみたいだ）
English: (I know I'm on the right track. I just need to keep going...)

Japanese: オレが‥‥ ライオンにおそわれた？
English: You're saying that I was attacked? By a lion!?

Japanese: そうです。
English: That's what I'm saying.

Japanese: いいですか、成歩堂さん。 オレ、アクロバットの芸人ですよ。
English: I'm sorry Mr. Wright, but I'm an acrobat.

Japanese: 猛獣使いじゃあるまいし‥‥ 逃げますよ。おそわれる前に。
English: I'm no animal tamer... If a lion was coming at me, I'd be running for the exit.

Japanese: “おそわれた”という言い方は 正しくないかもしれません。
English: I'm not sure if "attack" is the best word to be using.

Japanese: あなたは‥‥戦ったんです。 ライオンと。
English: You must have... battled the lion.

Japanese: ‥‥冗談が好きみたいですね。 どうしてオレがライオンと‥‥？
English: You seem to enjoy telling jokes... Why would I decide to battle a lion?

Japanese: あなたは、戦わなければ ならなかった。この人物のために。
English: Because you had to fight it. You had to fight it to save someone.

Japanese: さあ。どうですか、 アクロさん！
English: So how about it, Acro?

Japanese: ‥‥その前に、オレのほうから 聞いていいですか？
English: You mind if I ask one question first?

Japanese: な、なんですか？
English: Go ahead. What do you want to know?

Japanese: そんなモノのために、なぜ 戦わなければならないんです？
English: Why would I possibly battle the king of the jungle for something like that?

Japanese: え‥‥。 （どうやら、ちがうみたいだ‥‥）
English: Huh? (I must be going in the wrong direction.)

Japanese: ‥‥やれやれ。
English: Mr. Wright, I assure you that it would take more than that to get me to go after a lion.

Japanese: バット‥‥。
English: Bat...

Japanese: 半年前の“事故”‥‥ 弟さん、おキノドクでした。
English: It was a shame what happened to your brother six months ago.

Japanese: ‥‥彼を助けようと したんじゃないですか？
English: You tried to save him, didn't you?

Japanese: それで、その‥‥大ケガを。
English: And that's how you got that terrible injury.

Japanese: ‥‥トミーが‥‥話したんですか？
English: Moe... He must have told you...

Japanese: バットのことは、 トミーさんから聞きました。
English: Yes, we learned about Bat from Moe.

Japanese: でも彼は、あなたのことは 言いませんでしたよ。
English: But he didn't mention anything about you in regards to the accident.

Japanese: ‥‥ただちょっと、 口をすべらせたんです。
English: I suppose it was just a slip of the tongue on his part. That's how I figured it out.

Japanese: 口を‥‥？
English: A slip of the tongue?

Japanese: すげェコンビだったよ。 それが‥‥いっぺんに、パアだ。
English: Anyways, they were an incredible team... Cut down at the same time...

Japanese: “いっぺんに、パア”‥‥
English: "Cut down at the same time..." That was where he slipped, and that's how I figured it out.

Japanese: つまり、おふたりは同時に 事故にあわれたことになります。
English: You two ended up wrapped up in the same accident together, like always.

Japanese: ‥‥なるほど‥‥。
English: I see...

Japanese: でも、事故は事故です。 ‥‥だれのせいでもない。
English: But an accident is an accident. It wasn't anyone's fault...

Japanese: （アクロさんのロックは まだ、はずれていない‥‥）
English: (I still haven't broken Acro's Psyche-Lock...)

Japanese: （さらに深いヒミツが あるということか‥‥）
English: (He must have an incredibly deep seeded secret...)

Japanese: “だれのせいでもない”‥‥？ そうだったのでしょうか？
English: "It wasn't anyone's fault..." Do you care to explain more?

Japanese: ‥‥証拠も何もありません。 ただ、感じるんです。
English: Acro, I know you are still hiding something from me...

Japanese: アクロさんの、この人物に 対する“悪意”のようなものを‥‥
English: Maybe something you don't seem to like much is the reason you're being evasive.

Japanese: いかがですか？　アクロさん。
English: What about this, Acro?

Japanese: ‥‥あなたはフシギなヒトだ‥‥。
English: Are you aware how strange you seem sometimes?

Japanese: 何もかもを見通しているようで‥‥ じつは、そうでもない。
English: You seem to think you know what will happen before you do... But you're wrong.

Japanese: え。
English: What?

Japanese: あなたはやはり、 おわかりになっていないようだ。
English: It's clear you haven't come to grips with what's going on.

Japanese: （‥‥くそっ！ ここで引き返すワケには‥‥）
English: (Argh! There's no way I can back pedal away from this...)

Japanese: ‥‥み、ミリカ‥‥
English: R-Regina...

Japanese: いつも冷静なあなたが、彼女のコト を話すとき、ヘンに感情的になる。
English: You always seem calm and collected, until you start talking about her.

Japanese: “ザンコクなほど純粋”だ、とか。
English: Saying things like she is "cruel"...

Japanese: さすが弁護士さんだ。 ‥‥スルドイ目をしてるんですね。
English: Well done, Mr. Attorney. You've got quite the set of eagle eyes.

Japanese: ‥‥じつは、ぼくも おそわれたんですよ‥‥トラに。
English: You know... Her tiger tried to attack me...

Japanese: あなたが‥‥ラトーに？
English: Regent tried to attack you?

Japanese: しかも、２回。
English: Twice.

Japanese: ‥‥ハハッ。 ホンキなワケ、ないでしょう。
English: Hah hah hah... He wasn't serious I'm sure...

Japanese: まさか、ミリカが弟にレオンを けしかけた、って言うんですか？
English: You're not trying to insinuate that I believe she spurred on Léon to attack Bat, are you?

Japanese: レオンは、ヒトをおそう 命令など教えられていません。
English: Léon was never taught a command to attack people.

Japanese: ミリカに、 そんなコトはできませんよ。
English: Regina isn't capable of doing anything like that.

Japanese: それに‥‥彼女が、弟を殺そうと するはずがない。
English: Besides, Regina had no reason to want to hurt my little brother.

Japanese: ‥‥あの当時、ミリカと弟は とても仲がよかったんです。
English: Regina and Bat were such good friends back then...

Japanese: でも！　それでもあなたは、 ミリカさんをニクんでいる！
English: But you still hate Regina!

Japanese: ‥‥そう。証拠だってあります！
English: I've got proof of it!

Japanese: な‥‥なんですって‥‥！
English: What!? What are you talking about!?

Japanese: （今はまだ ‥‥ハッタリにすぎない‥‥）
English: (Doh! Maybe I overdid it again...)

Japanese: （でも、おそらく “これ”は、アクロさんが‥‥）
English: (But if I can hand something over to Acro, maybe it'll...)

Japanese: ‥‥あなたがミリカさんに 悪意を持っていた証拠は‥‥！
English: Here's proof that you had it out for Regina all along!

Japanese: ‥‥アクロさん？
English: Acro?

Japanese: オレとしては、ミリカよりも あなたをニクみますね。
English: You know, I think I hate you more than I ever hated her.

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: そんなもので、どうしてミリカを ニクまなくちゃならないんですか？
English: How could what you just showed me possibly prove that I hate Regina?

Japanese: う‥‥。 （まいったな‥‥ちがったらしい）
English: Uhhh... (It looks like I picked the wrong piece of evidence...)

Japanese: （しかし‥‥、 何かあるハズなんだ！）
English: (But there's gotta be something that works...)

Japanese: （‥‥よく、思い出せ‥‥）
English: (I know it...)

Japanese: そ‥‥それは‥‥ しょ、食堂に張ってあった‥‥？
English: T-This... Where'd you get it?

Japanese: 食堂の伝言板に 張ったのは、ミリカさんです。
English: Regina posted it on the bulletin board in the cafeteria.

Japanese: もともとは、彼女のポケット に入っていました。
English: Before that, it was in her pocket.

Japanese: あれかな。気がついたのは、 朝ゴハン持って行ったとき。
English: Hmm... I guess I noticed it was in there around breakfast time.

Japanese: アクロのゴハン、 いつもミリカが持って行くんだよ。
English: I always take Acro his breakfast in the morning.

Japanese: このメモ、あなたが書いて、 彼女のポケットに入れた‥‥。
English: You wrote this, and then you put it in her pocket...

Japanese: ちがいますか？
English: That's right, isn't it?

Japanese: そのとおりです。 ‥‥さすが、ですね。
English: That's right. Well done Mr. Wright...

Japanese: あ、見て！　見て！ マックスだよ。
English: Look! Look! It's Max!!

Japanese: ‥‥見る気がなくても 目に入ってくるよ。
English: Even when you don't want to see him... *poof* He's right in front of you.

Japanese: （“タチミ・サーカス”とは 書いてあるけど‥‥）
English: (Sure the sign says "Berry Big Circus"...)

Japanese: （どう見てもマックスひとりの カンバンだよな）
English: (But looking around... It might as well be Cirque du Galactica...)

Japanese: あのホッペのホシが シビれるよねー。
English: The stars on his cheek sure are dreamy...

Japanese: なるほどくんにも書いて あげようか？　サインペンで。
English: How about I draw a star on your cheek Nick? I've got a marker!

Japanese: ‥‥ぼくはいいや。
English: Nah! Nah! It's alright!

Japanese: チケットを売っているボックスだ。 サムいのに大変だな。
English: This is the Box Office where they sell all the tickets for the circus.

Japanese: あ。そういえば、こないだ パンフレット買い忘れちゃった。
English: They also sell programs. I forgot to buy one when we came to the circus last time.

Japanese: じゃあ、買ってくれば。今。
English: So then why don't you buy one now?

Japanese: そうしようかなあ。
English: Hmm... Sounds like a plan.

Japanese: ‥‥あ。今日はおサイフ 忘れてきちゃった。
English: Oh no... It looks like I forgot my wallet.

Japanese: スナオに“買って”って 言えよ！
English: If you want me to buy it for you, just ask me already.

Japanese: いやー、わるいね なるほどくん。
English: You know I'd never do that to you, Nick...

Japanese: この先なんだよね。 ‥‥サツジンゲンバ。
English: The murder took place right over there...

Japanese: やっぱり、 慣れることはできないね。
English: You never really get used to the feeling of being where someone's life was taken.

Japanese: うん。団長さん、 いいヒトだったみたいだし。
English: I know... The Ringmaster seems like he was such a good man.

Japanese: かわいそうだよね。 ‥‥みんな。
English: It so sad... For everyone.

Japanese: 売店だ。ホットドッグに ニクまん、あんまん、
English: It's a snack stand. They have hot dogs, hamburgers, and drinks... Not to mention...

Japanese: おしるこ、たこやきに‥‥ “冷たいかきごおり”だって！
English: Candy and popcorn... They've even got snow cones!!

Japanese: （な、なんでこの季節に かきごおりが‥‥）
English: (Who would eat snow cones in the middle of winter?)

Japanese: なるほどくん。 かきごおり食べたい。
English: Nick... Do you think we can buy some snow cones?

Japanese: ‥‥そのへんに いくらでもつもってるだろ。
English: Look around... There's tons of snow piled up all around here!

Japanese: わーい。‥‥って、 シロップかかってないじゃない！
English: YAY!! Wait a second... There's no syrup though! I want syrup!

Japanese: （かかってりゃ、いいのか‥‥？）
English: (Hopefully she doesn't notice that discolored snow in the corner. That's not syrup!)

Japanese: さ。さ。入り口はこっちだよ なるほどくん。
English: Nick... The entrance is right here.

Japanese: 今日はやってないぞ。 サーカス。
English: Maya. The circus is closed today. No clowns. No elephants. No shows.

Japanese: わかってるよ！
English: I know that!

Japanese: ホラ、入り口の横で、ゾウの ヒロミくんと写真が撮れるんだよ！
English: Nick... You can get your picture taken with Dali the elephant!

Japanese: 今日はいないぞ。 ヒロミくん。
English: There's no Dali. Not today.

Japanese: わかってるよ！
English: I know that too!

Japanese: ‥‥しょうがないなー。 おまわりさんと撮ろうか。写真。
English: Oh well... I'll just have to take a picture with whoever I stumble across...

Japanese: （ナニしにきたんだ‥‥）
English: (*sigh* It's not like we're here on business or anything...)

Japanese: おっきいねー、 サーカスのテントって。
English: The Berry Big Top is so... Very... B... *cough* Enormous...

Japanese: そうだなあ。コイツを見ると サーカスに来た、って感じがする。
English: Yep. One look at the huge tent looming over you and you realize "This is the circus!"

Japanese: うん！　わくわくするよね。
English: I know! I know! It really gets your blood pumping doesn't it!?

Japanese: サーカスのアドバルーンだ。
English: Those streamers do a nice job introducing the circus performers.

Japanese: “マックス・ギャラクティカと ゆかいな仲間たち”だって。
English: "Maximillion Galactica and his comical comrades!"

Japanese: ‥‥そういえば。
English: You know...

Japanese: アドバルーンて、あんまり 見かけないね。最近。
English: You don't really see too many streamers nowadays, do you?

Japanese: そうだねえ。
English: You're right... I haven't seen one in ages.

Japanese: 子どもがよじ登ったら あぶないからね。
English: I bet they stopped using them due to little kids climbing up to the top...

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんなら やりかねないな‥‥）
English: (Umm... I don't think that was why...)

Japanese: なんだろ。写真が並んでるよ、 ズラリと。
English: Nick... Look at all the photos lined up on the wall...

Japanese: このサーカスのレキシ‥‥ みたいなものらしいね。
English: It's like a guided tour of the circus' history.

Japanese: へええ。いいね、なんか 思い出がいっぱい、ってカンジ。
English: This is so cool! It seems like there were so many happy memories.

Japanese: そうだ！　あたしたちの事務所 でもやろうよ、それ。
English: Maybe we should do this sort of thing at our office?

Japanese: 無罪になった依頼人のヒトたちの 写真、じゃんじゃんかざるの。
English: We can put up pictures of all the clients who have been found not guilty.

Japanese: 有罪になった依頼人は？
English: What about the clients who were found guilty?

Japanese: うーん。エンギわるいから、 ハズしとこうか。
English: Umm... We'll just pretend like they don't exist. How's that?

Japanese: ダメだよ！　負けるコトなんて 考えちゃあ！
English: Nick! Now you've got me thinking about losing cases! Why'd you do that!?

Japanese: なんか、おんなじような ガクブチがならんでるよ。
English: All of these frames look the same.

Japanese: カンシャ状‥‥みたいだ。
English: They almost look like... Thank You cards.

Japanese: 団長さん、毎年、寄付を していたみたいだよ。
English: It looks like every year the Ringmaster made donations to charity...

Japanese: ‥‥ロボット・ピエロの 研究グループに。
English: To the Robot Clown Research Center...

Japanese: ダマされてたんじゃない？
English: You're kidding right?

Japanese: そ、そういうコト言っちゃ いけないと思うな。
English: What!? They may be a perfectly reputable charity in the field of advanced tomfoolery!

Japanese: 団長さん、ここで お化粧してたんだね。
English: This is where the Ringmaster applied his makeup.

Japanese: 化粧品のビンがならんでいる。 ハデなのばっかりだ。
English: It's quite a collection of the most understated colors. Shocking Pink for example.

Japanese: “１００パーセント天然素材の マスカラ”って書いてあるよ。
English: This one says it is "100% All Natural Organic Mascara."

Japanese: こっちも、“おハダにやさしい ドーラン”だって。
English: And this one says "Sensitive enough for a baby, strong enough for a mime."

Japanese: ‥‥いろいろ気をつかうヒト だったみたいだね。
English: The Ringmaster must have been really concerned about skin care... Very metrosexual.

Japanese: ‥‥イガイに安っぽいね。 団長さんのデスク。
English: This is strange... Everything else looks nice, but this desk looks old and cheap.

Japanese: 大きな写真立てがあるぞ。 写ってるのは‥‥
English: There's a really big photo on the desk...

Japanese: 団長さんと、ミリカちゃん ‥‥か。
English: It's a picture of Regina and her father, the Ringmaster.

Japanese: 団長さん、ホントにかわいがって たんだ。ミリカちゃんのコト。
English: He really loved her didn't he? Regina was lucky to have such a wonderful father.

Japanese: わあ。かわいいトロフィーが いっぱいあるよ。
English: Nick! Look at all the cute trophies!!

Japanese: すごいね。いろんな賞を とってるんだ、このサーカス。
English: Indeed. Just look at all the awards this circus has won...

Japanese: なになに。
English: Like...

Japanese: “町内クイズ王” “団長杯ゴルフコンペ”
English: "All County Quiz Champions"... "Ringmaster's Association Mini Golf Master"...

Japanese: “大食い選手権優勝” “ペット自慢グランプリ”
English: "Beer Belly Balloon Bounce Champ"... "Pet Grooming Grand Prix"...

Japanese: ‥‥こぢんまりした ヒトだったんだね。
English: Wow. The Ringmaster was multi-talented in ways I could have never imagined...

Japanese: 来客用のテーブルだ。
English: This was the table he used when visitors came to see him.

Japanese: ‥‥なんか、ドロが こびりついてるよ。テーブルの上。
English: Eww... There's mud caked on the table.

Japanese: ぎょうぎの悪い客が クツのまま足を乗せたんだろうね。
English: Someone with terrible manners must have put their shoes up on the table!

Japanese: ダメだよ。なるほどくんは そんなコトしちゃ。
English: Nick! Don't even think about providing a re-enactment for me.

Japanese: しないよ！
English: I wasn't! How uncouth do you think I am anyway?

Japanese: みんなサーカスのポスターだ。
English: Look at all the stars on this poster...

Japanese: 今までの公演で 使ってきたものだろう。
English: This must have been the poster they used to promote their public appearances.

Japanese: うーん。やっぱりこれからは ポスターだね。
English: Posters are the way to go aren't they?

Japanese: ‥‥なにが？
English: What do you mean?

Japanese: ウチの事務所も作ろうよ！ ポスター。
English: We should make posters to promote our law firm.

Japanese: “手に汗ニギるスリルと サスペンス！　そして恐怖！”
English: "Spine tingling legal action! Mind numbing legalese! You will say 'wow'!"

Japanese: “絶体絶命！　イノチをかけた 法廷ショー”‥‥とか。どう？
English: Or perhaps, "Hold it! Don't miss out on a stunning life or death courtroom thrill ride!"

Japanese: 依頼人、来なくなるぞ。
English: With those taglines, our law firm would sink faster than the Titanic...

Japanese: ヨゴれた食器が つみ上げられている。
English: There are dirty dishes all over the place...

Japanese: かたづけるのが メンドウなのだろう。
English: They must have been too lazy to clean up after themselves.

Japanese: “メンドウなのだろう” じゃないよ！　なるほどくん！
English: They weren't "too lazy"!

Japanese: ホラ。あたしたちで かたづけようよ！
English: Just for that Nick, you and I are going to clean up for them!

Japanese: なな、なんで ぼくたちが‥‥！ up...!?
English: W-Why do we have to clean

Japanese: きたない食堂は ユルせないもん、あたし。
English: One, because you were rude. Two, because I hate dirty kitchens.

Japanese: い、いやホラ。 殺人事件があったわけだから。
English: B-But... This is a crime scene!

Japanese: 現場はキチンと保存 しておかないと。
English: We can't clean it up! We have to preserve it for evidence!!

Japanese: うう‥‥。 気になるなあ、もう。
English: Arrrggghh... You know I really hate dirty kitchens!!

Japanese: （やれやれ。ぼくも かたづけはニガテだからな‥‥）
English: (Not as much as I hate cleaning dirty kitchens!)

Japanese: 事件があった日のまま、 ってカンジだね。
English: It looks like they've left it exactly as it was on the night of the murder.

Japanese: それにしても ちらかってるよねえ。
English: They didn't seem too worried about cleaning up the dishes, did they?

Japanese: ‥‥なんだか、おなか すいてきちゃったよ。
English: I know... Looking at all these dishes is making me hungry!

Japanese: ソトの売店で たこやき食べようか。
English: Let's go get a burger at the snack stand outside.

Japanese: 事件がカイケツしたらね。
English: Once we're finished with the case.

Japanese: よし！　いっちょ、やるよ！ なるほどくん！
English: Alright! Then let's get this over with Nick!! Here we go!! Whoahh!

Japanese: （キアイじゅうぶんだ‥‥）
English: (Where's the mute button when you need it?)

Japanese: 伝言板だ。 ‥‥連絡用かな。
English: Ah, a bulletin board... For... Umm... Bulletins.

Japanese: 特に手がかりになるような ことは書いてないな。
English: It doesn't look like there are any useful clues posted here.

Japanese: つまんないなー。 ‥‥なんか書いておこうか？
English: Boring... Maybe we should leave a juicy tidbit for someone to read!

Japanese: え。ナニを？
English: Juicy?

Japanese: ニセの手がかり。‥‥そうだなあ。 たとえば“犯行声明”とか。
English: You know... Like a fake clue! Hmm... Maybe something like... "Message from the Killer!"

Japanese: やめとこう。イトノコ刑事が ホンキにするぞ。
English: Give it up Maya... You know Gumshoe will take it all seriously.

Japanese: イスが倒れてる。
English: The chair's been knocked over.

Japanese: 見たまんまだね。
English: That's what it looks like.

Japanese: どうも、だれかが 暴れたみたいに見えるな。
English: It almost looks as if someone knocked it over in a struggle, or used it for self defense...

Japanese: ラトーがゴハン食べたんじゃ ないかな。ここで。
English: Or maybe Regent just likes to eat his dinner in the cafeteria?

Japanese: ‥‥なんだろ？　この台。 何も乗ってないけど。
English: This is strange... There's nothing on top of this stand.

Japanese: “マックス”って書いてあるよ。 専用の席かな。
English: Look here... Max is written on it. It must be his VIP table.

Japanese: ‥‥さすがに、ちょっと せまいんじゃないか？
English: Isn't a bit small to be a VIP table? You won't be putting a ten course meal on this.

Japanese: ラーメンぐらいなら 食べられそうだけどね。
English: Well, he could still eat hamburgers, right?

Japanese: 『食堂内は清潔に！』 って書いてあるよ。
English: "Keep Our Cafeteria Clean!"

Japanese: そのワリに、きたないな。
English: Doesn't seem like anyone ever read this sign, huh?

Japanese: “せいけつに”って、 ひらがなで書いたらいいのにね。
English: Maybe they should make it easier to understand... "Clean it or die!"

Japanese: 怒られるぞ、 ここのサーカスのヒトに。
English: Well, that would definitely make them clean up!

Japanese: この奥はキッチンに なってるみたいだ。
English: The kitchen is over here.

Japanese: 食事係は当番制なのかな。 リストが張ってある。
English: Everyone must have taken turns cooking. There's a duty list posted here.

Japanese: あたしもやってみたいな。 食事当番。
English: I wouldn't mind trying my hand at cooking for this many people.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんの必殺ラーメン。 ‥‥食べてみたいでしょ？
English: Maya's Killer Hamburger... You'd try it, wouldn't you?

Japanese: ヨゴれた食器が つみ上げられている。
English: There are dirty dishes all over the place...

Japanese: かたづけるのが メンドウなのだろう。
English: It must have been too hectic that night to clean up.

Japanese: “メンドウなのだろう” じゃないよ！　なるほどくん！
English: You know I can't stand a mess Nick!

Japanese: ホラ。あたしたちで かたづけようよ！
English: I think you and I should clean this place up for them!

Japanese: そうだよ！　ニイちゃんたちで さっさとかたづけな！
English: Yay! You two are going to help us restore order to this cafeteria!?

Japanese: ナニ言ってるんですか！ 手伝ってくださいよ！
English: What are you talking about? You're gonna help too!

Japanese: いや、ピエロはホレ。
English: Clowns can't clean!! We're here to throw pies, not clean them up!

Japanese: 笑いモノが専門なの。 洗いモノはせんモンなの。
English: I'm a bust-a-gut boy, not a bus boy!

Japanese: ‥‥なーんて。あひゃ。あひゃ。 あひゃあひゃひゃ。あひゃらひゃ。
English: Get it... It was two jokes in one! I'm fat and funny! Get it!? Aha! Aha! Aha!

Japanese: ぼくも、弁護が専門だから。
English: Yes. Moe. We got it.

Japanese: ううう‥‥
English: Uhhh...

Japanese: 事件があった日のまま、 ってカンジだね。
English: It looks like they've left it exactly as it was on the night of the murder.

Japanese: あの日はオレっちが 食事当番でさあ。
English: I was in charge of the cooking that day.

Japanese: トクベツメニューだったワケよ。
English: It was going to be a special menu.

Japanese: へええ。 どんなメニューだったんですか？
English: Really? What were you going to cook?

Japanese: トミーどんぶり。
English: Moe Moo Burgers.

Japanese: ‥‥なんですか、それ。
English: What are those?

Japanese: ユカイでユーモラスだけど、 どこかホロ苦い‥‥
English: A beefy burger filled with laughs and a sauce that tastes like my jokes.

Japanese: そんな味。
English: They're outstanding.

Japanese: （想像をこえた どんぶりらしいな‥‥）
English: (So those burgers leave just as bad of a taste in your mouth as his jokes then.)

Japanese: イスが倒れてる。
English: The chair's been knocked over.

Japanese: 見たまんまだね。
English: That's what it looks like.

Japanese: この光景をみるとサ。 思いだしちまうんだよな。
English: Looking at all this brings back lots of memories.

Japanese: 何をですか？
English: What do you mean?

Japanese: オレっちのプロポーズ。 ‥‥食堂で、馬乗りのムスメにさ。
English: I proposed here... To a girl on horseback.

Japanese: 若いコロの思い出だよ。
English: Of course, I was much younger back then.

Japanese: へええ。そのプロポーズ、 どうなったんですか。
English: So what happened? What was her answer?

Japanese: うん。馬乗りで殴られた。 テレてたんだろうな。あの子。
English: Well... Let's just say that my love was put out to pasture.

Japanese: 『食堂内は清潔に！』 って書いてあるよ。
English: "Keep Our Cafeteria Clean!"

Japanese: いやいやいや。 よく見なよ、おぢょーさん！
English: Take a good look at that sign girlie.

Japanese: ‥‥あ！ 『食堂内は清潔に！？』だ。
English: Huh? It says... "Keep Our Cafeteria Clean!?"

Japanese: オレっちが“？”を 書きこんだんだけどサ。
English: I added a question mark on there...

Japanese: ‥‥それからだよ。 食堂がこんなになったのは。
English: I'm thinking that might be why the cafeteria is so dirty.

Japanese: この奥はキッチンに なってるみたいだ。
English: The kitchen is over here.

Japanese: あ！　そうそう！
English: Oh yeah! Oh yeah!

Japanese: キッチンをネタにした、 とびきりホットなギャグがあるぜ！
English: I wrote a new joke about the kitchen...

Japanese: えーと‥‥なんだっけなあ‥‥
English: Let's see... How did that joke go... Umm...

Japanese: （まさか‥‥“キッチンは キチンとかたづけよう”）
English: (Lemme guess... "What did the chef say to the chicken before dinner? Knife to meet ya!")

Japanese: （なんてことは‥‥）
English: (It's gotta be something dumb like that...)

Japanese: ぴんぽんぴんぽん！ それそれ！　あひゃ。やるね！
English: What did the chef say to the chicken before dinner? Knife to meet ya!

Japanese: くやしいなー。それは 思いつかなかったよ！
English: That's a great one Moe. I would have never thought of that.

Japanese: うわー、大きなライト。
English: Whoa! Those lights are huge!

Japanese: 落ちてきたら死んじゃうね こりゃあ。
English: If they fell from the ceiling, you'd be going to the circus in the sky...

Japanese: 人生なんて、はかないものだねえ なるほどくん。
English: Life is so fragile, isn't it Nick?

Japanese: ま、まあね。
English: It can be snuffed out in an instant by falling gargantuan lights.

Japanese: 死ぬまでに、いっぱい おいしいもの食べとかないと。
English: It's so fragile that I've decided to eat as much good food as I can before I die.

Japanese: たこやきとか。
English: Like hamburgers...

Japanese: ソトの売店、 今日もやってないぞ。
English: Apparently you didn't notice the big sign that said "closed" by the snack stand.

Japanese: うう‥‥ザンネン。
English: Awww... It's closed!?

Japanese: あ、あれ！　あそこから 出てきたんだよね、マックス！
English: Hey! Look! That's where Max makes his grand appearance!

Japanese: ああ、あれは カッコよかったな。
English: It's "grand" alright.

Japanese: あたしも出てみたい。 あそこから。
English: I want to try entering court like that.

Japanese: （言うと思った‥‥）
English: (How'd I know that she'd say that...)

Japanese: 客席、けっこう遠いね。
English: The seats are so far away from the ring...

Japanese: うん。でも、かなり ヒトが入れそうだな。
English: But they make up for it with the amount of people that can see a show.

Japanese: ね。ね。なるほどくん。
English: Hey Nick...

Japanese: マンインのお客さんが いっせいに深呼吸したら、
English: Do you think that if every person in the audience screamed all at once,

Japanese: ペコッ、てヘコむかなあ。 ‥‥テント。
English: the vibration they'd create could cause the big top to collapse?

Japanese: あるいはね。
English: I'm glad I don't pay you to come up with this stuff...

Japanese: うわー、大きなライト。
English: Whoa! Those lights are huge!

Japanese: ミリカ、ライト大好き。
English: I love lights!!

Japanese: ミリカにライトが集まると、 みんな拍手してくれるの。
English: Whenever I appear under the spotlight, everyone claps for me!

Japanese: だってミリカちゃん、 カワイイもんねー。
English: That's because everyone knows that you're cute!

Japanese: そんなコトないよ。 真宵ちゃんだって。
English: No... I'm not cute. You're cute!

Japanese: えッ！
English: Me!?

Japanese: きっと、なれるよ。 ヒロインに。
English: Of course! I'm sure you'd make an incredible heroine!

Japanese: きゃあ！　ホント！
English: Really!? You think so!?

Japanese: ね。ね。じゃあさ。 なるほどくんは。どう？
English: Nick! Did you hear that!? Me!! A heroine!! What about Nick, Regina?

Japanese: うーん。どうかなあ。
English: Hmm... Nick...

Japanese: ダメだって。
English: He's no hero, is he?

Japanese: （‥‥ちょっとキズついた）
English: (Ouch... Thanks a lot Maya!)

Japanese: あ、あれ！　あそこから 出てきたんだよね、マックス！
English: Look! That's where Max comes out during the show!!

Japanese: ああ、あれは カッコよかったな。
English: I've got to admit, that was a pretty cool effect!

Japanese: あのね。今度のステージ、 ミリカもあそこから出るんだよ。
English: We're planning for me to start coming out of the lion during the show.

Japanese: へえ。そいつはすごいね！
English: That's great Regina!

Japanese: うん。ラトーの背中に 乗って、飛び出すの。
English: Yeah. I will ride on Regent's back and jump out of the lion's mouth.

Japanese: ううう。あたしも やってみたいよー。
English: I wanna try it too!

Japanese: なるほどくんの背中に 乗って、飛び出すの。
English: I'll ride on Nick's back and jump out of the lion's mouth!

Japanese: （なんでぼくなんだよ！）
English: (Sometimes I wonder about this girl...)

Japanese: 客席、けっこう遠いね。
English: The seats are kinda far away don't you think?

Japanese: うん。でも、かなり ヒトが入れそうだな。
English: They are, but it also means that lots of people can fit in the big top.

Japanese: ５００人ぐらい 入るんだよ。お客さん。
English: He's right. We can fit 500 people into a show.

Japanese: へええ。
English: Five hundred... That's amazing.

Japanese: その上を飛びまわるの、 すごく気持ちいいんだって。
English: Flying around above that many people is a "real rush".

Japanese: マックスが言ってたよ。
English: At least that's what Max said.

Japanese: ‥‥特に手がかりになるような ものは、入ってない。
English: There doesn't seem to be anything here that can help us... Not a single clue.

Japanese: じゃあ聞くけどさ、 なるほどくん。
English: You know I've been meaning to ask you...

Japanese: “特に手がかりになるようなモノ” って、ナニ？
English: What exactly do you mean when you say "clue"? What are you looking for?

Japanese: 血まみれのナイフとか。
English: A bloody chainsaw for instance...

Japanese: うーん。そりゃま、 入ってないだろうねえ‥‥。
English: Well there's definitely not one of those here...

Japanese: あ。あそこにあるの、 ロープだよね！
English: Hey! It's a rope!

Japanese: うん。ツナわたりの ロープなのかな‥‥？
English: Probably for tightrope walking...

Japanese: おかしいなあ。
English: That's a bit strange...

Japanese: こないだのプログラム、 ツナわたりなんか、なかったよ。
English: There weren't any tightrope walkers in the show when we saw it.

Japanese: あ、ハシゴだ。
English: Ah, a ladder.

Japanese: それはキャタツだよ。
English: It's just a stepladder.

Japanese: どうちがうの？ 同じようなものじゃない。
English: What's the difference? They do the same thing, right?

Japanese: もっとホンシツを見ようよ、 なるほどくん。
English: I think you should stick with the basic facts of the matter.

Japanese: ははは。
English: Oh... Uh... OK.

Japanese: （‥‥どこかで、似たような 会話をかわしたような‥‥）
English: (It's not even worth arguing with her on this one.)

Japanese: 団長さんの死体は、 ここで見つかった。
English: The Ringmaster's body was found here.

Japanese: 木箱をかかえるようにして 倒れていたらしいけど、
English: I heard that the way he fell over made him look like he was carrying a wooden box...

Japanese: ‥‥いったい、ここで 何があったんだろう。
English: But what in the world really happened here?

Japanese: シートでくるまれた資材だ。 古いカンバンなどが置かれている。
English: Something's covered up by this tarp... An old sign perhaps.

Japanese: 気をつけて！　なるほどくん！
English: Nick! Look out!

Japanese: そこのカゲに、ハンニンが かくれてるかも！
English: They've got the killer trapped behind that tarp!

Japanese: つまんないの。
English: What? It wasn't funny?

Japanese: ちょっとぐらい、おどろいて くれてもいいのにさ。
English: You know it wouldn't have hurt you to look even a little bit surprised.

Japanese: （‥‥じつは、ビックリして 声も出なかったんだけど）
English: (Whew! She didn't notice that I was too scared to talk!)

Japanese: エアコンの室外機だ。 しばらく使われていないようだ。
English: This is the condenser for the air conditioner. It hasn't been used in a while.

Japanese: ええっ！　どうして そんなコトわかるの！
English: What!? How'd you figure that out!? You're not making things up now are you!?

Japanese: そ、そんなにおどろかれても こまるけど。
English: Hold your horses! It was easy to figure out!

Japanese: 雪は、事件の前にやんでいただろ。
English: It was snowing before the murder, right?

Japanese: そのときつもった雪が、 まだとけていないから。
English: Well, look at the condenser. The snow is still piled on top of it.

Japanese: うおッ！　すごい！ なるほどくん、名探偵だッ！
English: Wow! Nick! I'm impressed. Maybe you should be a P.I. instead of an attorney.

Japanese: この街灯、事件のあったときも ついていたんだよね。
English: This safety light was on at the time of the murder, wasn't it?

Japanese: そうだなあ。
English: That's what they say.

Japanese: （宿舎には、芸人さんたちが いたんだから‥‥）
English: (There were performers in the lodging house, so it makes sense that it was on.)

Japanese: （だれかに目撃されるキケン性は じゅうぶんあったはずだ‥‥）
English: (I bet the killer was worried that someone would spot them during the crime...)

Japanese: それにしても、スゴいよねえ。 トミーさん。
English: You've really gotta give it to Moe...

Japanese: 毎日まいにち、バナナの皮で １００回ころんでるんだもん。
English: Slipping on a banana 100 times a day. That's incredible.

Japanese: ‥‥あたしも がんばってみようかな。
English: Maybe I should try giving it a shot myself.

Japanese: 手始めに、お手玉から。
English: I guess I'll start out by juggling.

Japanese: （まずは、霊媒のほうを なんとかしたほうが‥‥）
English: (Maybe you should start with spirit channeling... Just a thought...)

Japanese: 見て！　見て！　ピエロ・グッズが たくさんあるよ！
English: Tee hee... Clown equipment is so funny looking!

Japanese: 玉乗りの玉に、一輪車、 トランポリンまである。
English: He's got a balancing ball, a unicycle... He's even got a trampoline!!

Japanese: ‥‥みんなコワれてるけど。
English: But they're all broken.

Japanese: やっぱり、チャレンジするのが ダイジなんだね！
English: Well, you've got to give them all a shot. Trying is the most important thing!!

Japanese: （何かひとつでも、 モノになってるのだろうか？）
English: (He sure seems to have tried them all... But were any of these useful?)

Japanese: なにやら、むずかしそうな 本がつんである。
English: Moe seems to be a voracious reader. Look at all the hard books he has here.

Japanese: “ピエロ学・研究序説入門” “シャレたユーモア入門”
English: "Clowns for Dummies", "The Joke's On You",

Japanese: “部下のココロをつかむ入門” “ゆかいなジョーク入門”
English: "Treat Your Peons Right", And the classic "Funny Jokes Are Funny."

Japanese: うーん。ベンキョウ熱心だねえ。
English: Wow. Moe is very studious.

Japanese: “シャレたユーモア”ねえ‥‥。
English: "The Joke's On You"... Huh?

Japanese: なんか、ヘコんでるぞ、 てんじょうが。
English: Look at the ceiling... It looks like someone punched a hole in it!

Japanese: ホントだ。 ‥‥どうしたんだろうね。
English: You're right. I wonder what happened?

Japanese: （ナゾを解く手がかりは マドぎわにありそうだな）
English: (Hmm... I don't even want to imagine what goes on in here.)

Japanese: いやあ、このニンジンも コダワリだったんだねえ。
English: These carrots look like they are the pick of the crop, don't they?

Japanese: そもそも、どうして ニンジンなんだろ？
English: I still don't get why he went with carrots...

Japanese: うーん‥‥そうだねえ。 こういうスイリはどう？
English: Hmm... What do you think about this theory?

Japanese: まだムカシ、サーカスが もうからなかったころ、
English: A long time ago, the circus was down on its luck.

Japanese: おなかがすいたトミーさんは、 おウマさんのエサをくすねてたの。
English: Moe was so hungry that he had to steal the horses food to survive.

Japanese: それがクセになっちゃった‥‥ なーんて。あはははは。
English: But he got addicted to their orangey goodness... Ha ha ha ha ha.

Japanese: （‥‥なんか、笑えない リアリティを感じるな‥‥）
English: (I'd laugh, but I think Maya might not be too far from the truth.)

Japanese: ベッドの上に、ハデなパジャマが ハデにぬぎちらかされている。
English: Moe's got an excellent pair of pajamas... Laid on his bed in an excellent manner.

Japanese: パジャマって言っても、 ケッキョク衣装だけどね。
English: They're not actually pajamas. They're actually costumes.

Japanese: うーん。プロフェッショナル、 ってカンジだよねえ。
English: Uh huh... I guess you could say that they're professional sleepwear.

Japanese: ピエロの衣装がズラリ。 ある種、ブキミなながめだ。
English: All those clown costumes lined up like that... I dunno about you, but it's creepy.

Japanese: ひゃああ。スゴい コレクションだなあ。
English: Look at the collection he's got! It's incredible!

Japanese: 世界のピエロの衣装だね きっと。
English: It must be a collection of clown costumes from around the world.

Japanese: あッ、そうだ！
English: Oh! I almost forgot!

Japanese: な、なんだよ。 （またロクでもないコトを‥‥）
English: What is it now? (She better not want me to try one of those on!)

Japanese: あたしも集めようかなあ。
English: I was thinking of starting a costume collection myself!

Japanese: 世界の霊媒師の装束。 事務所にズラリとかざるの！
English: I'll call it "World Spirit Channels"... We can display it in our office!

Japanese: なんでぼくの事務所に かざるんだよ！
English: In OUR office!? As soon as you start paying the bills, you can say that.

Japanese: トミーさん、ここから 目撃したんだよね、事件。
English: This is where Moe witnessed the crime.

Japanese: ああ。‥‥トンでもない シロモノをね。
English: Well, this is where Moe witnessed some craziness...

Japanese: （まったく‥‥ハンニンが 空を飛んだ、なんて‥‥）
English: (I mean, c'mon... A flying criminal... Jeez...)

Japanese: （ふつうの裁判官なら 信じないぞ‥‥）
English: (If we had a normal judge, he'd never believe something like that...)

Japanese: サルのルーサーが 遊ぶための器具だろう。
English: Money must use this to swing around on...

Japanese: なるほどくんも あそんでいいよ。
English: Go ahead Nick. I know you want to ride on it too!

Japanese: ‥‥また今度にする。
English: Maybe later...

Japanese: バーベルに、ブルワーカー ‥‥とかいったっけ。
English: Hmm... He's got a barbell.

Japanese: おもに、上半身をきたえるための トレーニング機器だ。
English: Look around... Everything he's got is for upper body training.

Japanese: ‥‥テレビの通信販売で よく見かけるラインナップだな。
English: Wait a second... These are the same machines I see on TV all the time!

Japanese: なっ、なるほどくん！
English: Hey, Nick!

Japanese: どうした？
English: What?

Japanese: も、持てないよッ！ このバーベル！
English: I don't have this one! This barbell here is a new model!

Japanese: ‥‥ムリしないほうがいいよ。 ギックリ腰になるから。
English: Don't overdo it Maya. You don't want to end up a muscle woman.

Japanese: あーあ。コワれてるよ このカゴ。
English: Hey... The net's ripped.

Japanese: ルーサーくんだね、 こんなコトするの。
English: Money must be prone to breaking things... He's hardcore.

Japanese: ‥‥？　こわれてるって、 どこが？
English: Umm... The net looks fine to me. You really think that he plays basketball?

Japanese: え。だって、やぶけてるよ。 カゴの底が。
English: I think so... Monkeys live life above the rim you know.

Japanese: もしかして‥‥ 知らないの？　バスケット。
English: You're joking right? You think the monkey has got proverbial "game"?

Japanese: バスケット？　知ってるよ。 日本語で“カゴ”のコトでしょ？
English: Of course. That monkey doesn't fake the funk on a nasty dunk.

Japanese: （‥‥深く追求するのは また今度にしよう‥‥）
English: (Well, a prehensile tail might be an unfair advantage...)

Japanese: ひゃあ。ガラクタの山だよ。
English: Eww... It's a huge pile of trash!

Japanese: これがルーサーの お宝、ってわけか。
English: One man's trash is another monkey's treasure.

Japanese: たしかに、ピカピカ光るモノ ばっかりだね。
English: I guess you're right. Look! They're all shiny things.

Japanese: オートバイのマフラーまで あるぞ。
English: He's even got a motorcycle exhaust.

Japanese: あの楽器、なんていったっけ。 ‥‥ああ。チューバだチューバ。
English: What do they call this thing? Oh... A tuba right?

Japanese: テューバだよ、なるほどくん。
English: Of course it's a tuba, silly! You didn't know that!?

Japanese: ‥‥あれ。 ミリカちゃんの写真があるよ！
English: Hey... It's a picture of Regina.

Japanese: うーん。彼女もある種、 キラキラしてるから。
English: Well, she is a shining beauty.

Japanese: そういうコトなのか‥‥？
English: No objections here.

Japanese: ベッドはキレイに ととのえられている。
English: This bed is incredibly well made. It's almost like a maid made it up.

Japanese: せんたくものまでが、 キチンとたたまれている。
English: Even the laundry on top of the bed is folded perfectly.

Japanese: これがあたりまえだよ なるほどくん。
English: Nick, there's nothing unusual about that at all. It's how things are supposed to be!

Japanese: ‥‥ぼくにはできそうもないな。
English: Can't a man respect another man for doing something said man cannot do?

Japanese: あ。おサルさんの カレンダーだよ。
English: Hehe... It's a monkey calendar!

Japanese: よっぽどサル好きなんだろうね、 アクロさん。
English: Acro must really love monkeys.

Japanese: マドのそとから、 サーカスのテントが見える。
English: You can see the Big Top from the window here.

Japanese: テントの上、雪が つもってないね。
English: There is no snow on top of the Big Top... Kinda weird don't you think?

Japanese: テントの中、あったかかった からね。とけちゃったんだろ。
English: The inside of the tent is warm so any snow that lands on top probably melts.

Japanese: そうだねえ。テント、雪が つもったらつぶれちゃうもんね。
English: I guess you're right. The snow probably just slides right off.

Japanese: （今はまだ、証拠が 足りないのかもしれない）
English: (I don't think I have enough evidence yet...)

Japanese: （もう少し調査をして、 材料を集めてみるか‥‥）
English: (I should investigate and gather some more clues before I try again...)

Japanese: ‥なるほどくん‥
English: ...Mr. Nick...

Japanese: ‥これ以上つづけると、 ココロがこわれてしまいます‥
English: ...If you push yourself any more, your soul will shatter...

Japanese: ‥もう一度、冷静になって 考えなおしましょう‥
